// chunker.js — Paragraph-aware text chunking with sentence-level fallback
2 



  3
  
  
import { generateUUID } from './utils.js';



  4
  
  



  5
  
  
/**



  6
  
  
 * Split text into semantically-aware chunks with overlap.



  7
  
  
 *



  8
  
  
 * Strategy:



  9
  
  
 * 1. Split by paragraphs (double newlines) — preserves semantic boundaries



  10
  
  
 * 2. Merge small paragraphs until chunk limit is reached



  11
  
  
 * 3. For oversized paragraphs, fall back to sentence-level splitting



  12
  
  
 *



  13
  
  
 * This works well with last_token pooling because the last token of a coherent



  14
  
  
 * paragraph captures better semantic meaning than an arbitrary character split.



  15
  
  
 *



  16
  
  
 * @param {string} text - The full document text



  17
  
  
 * @param {Object} options



  18
  
  
 * @param {number} options.maxTokens - Max tokens per chunk (default 512)



  19
  
  
 * @param {number} options.overlapPercent - Overlap percentage (default 12%)



  20
  
  
 * @param {string} options.sourceFile - Source filename for metadata



  21
  
  
 * @returns {Array} Array of chunk objects with id, content, and metadata



  22
  
  
 */



  23
  
  
export function chunkText(text, options = {}) {



  24
  
  
  const {



  25
  
  
    maxTokens = 512,



  26
  
  
    overlapPercent = 12,



  27
  
  
    sourceFile = 'unknown',



  28
  —
  
  } = options;



  29
  
  



  30
  —
  
  const maxChars = maxTokens * 4; // ~4 chars per token heuristic



  31
  —
  
  const overlapChars = Math.floor(maxChars * overlapPercent / 100);



  32
  
  



  33
  
  
  // Strategy 1: Split by paragraphs (double newlines)



  34
  —
  
  const paragraphs = text.split(/\n\n+/).filter(p => p.trim().length > 0);



  35
  —
  
  const chunks = [];



  36
  —
  
  let currentChunk = '';



  37
  —
  
  let charOffset = 0;



  38
  
  



  39
  —
  
  for (const paragraph of paragraphs) {



  40
  —
  
    const trimmed = paragraph.trim();



  41
  —
  



  42
  —
  
    if (trimmed.length <= maxChars) {



  43
  —
  
      // Paragraph fits in a chunk



  44
  —
  
      if (currentChunk && (currentChunk.length + trimmed.length) <= maxChars) {



  45
  —
  
        currentChunk += '\n\n' + trimmed;



  46
  —
  
      } else {



  47
  —
  
        // Save current chunk and start new one



  48
  —
  
        if (currentChunk.trim()) {



  49
  —
  
          chunks.push({



  50
  —
  
            content: currentChunk.trim(),



  51
  —
  
            charOffset: charOffset,



  52
  —
  
            charLength: currentChunk.trim().length,



  53
  —
  
          });



  54
  —
  
        }



  55
  —
  
        currentChunk = trimmed;



  56
  —
  
        charOffset += currentChunk.length + 2;



  57
  —
  
      }



  58
  —
  
    } else {



  59
  —
  
      // Paragraph too long — split by sentences



  60
  —
  
      if (currentChunk.trim()) {



  61
  —
  
        chunks.push({



  62
  —
  
          content: currentChunk.trim(),



  63
  —
  
          charOffset: charOffset,



  64
  —
  
          charLength: currentChunk.trim().length,



  65
  —
  
        });



  66
  —
  
      }



  67
  —
  



  68
  —
  
      const sentenceChunks = splitBySentences(trimmed, maxChars, overlapChars);



  69
  —
  
      for (const sc of sentenceChunks) {



  70
  —
  
        chunks.push({



  71
  —
  
          ...sc,



  72
  —
  
          charOffset: charOffset + sc.charOffset,



  73
  —
  
        });



  74
  —
  
      }



  75
  —
  
      charOffset += trimmed.length + 2;



  76
  —
  
      currentChunk = '';



  77
  —
  
    }



  78
  —
  
  }



  79
  
  



  80
  
  
  // Don't forget the last chunk



  81
  —
  
  if (currentChunk.trim()) {



  82
  —
  
    chunks.push({



  83
  —
  
      content: currentChunk.trim(),



  84
  —
  
      charOffset: charOffset,



  85
  —
  
      charLength: currentChunk.trim().length,



  86
  —
  
    });



  87
  —
  
  }



  88
  
  



  89
  
  
  // Add metadata to each chunk



  90
  —
  
  return chunks.map((chunk, index) => ({



  91
  —
  
    id: generateUUID(),



  92
  —
  
    content: chunk.content,



  93
  —
  
    metadata: {



  94
  —
  
      sourceFile,



  95
  —
  
      chunkIndex: index,



  96
  —
  
      charOffset: chunk.charOffset,



  97
  —
  
      charLength: chunk.charLength,



  98
  —
  
    },



  99
  —
  
  }));



  100
  
  
}



  101
  
  



  102
  
  
/**



  103
  
  
 * Split a long text block by sentence boundaries with overlap.



  104
  
  
 * @param {string} text - The text to split



  105
  
  
 * @param {number} maxChars - Max characters per chunk



  106
  
  
 * @param {number} overlapChars - Characters of overlap between chunks



  107
  
  
 * @returns {Array} Array of { content, charOffset } objects



  108
  
  
 */



  109
  
  
function splitBySentences(text, maxChars, overlapChars) {



  110
  
  
  // Split by sentence boundaries (.!? followed by space or end)



  111
  —
  
  const sentences = text.match(/[^.!?]+[.!?]+[\s]*/g) || [text];



  112
  —
  
  const chunks = [];



  113
  —
  
  let current = '';



  114
  
  



  115
  —
  
  for (const sentence of sentences) {



  116
  —
  
    if (current.length + sentence.length <= maxChars) {



  117
  —
  
      current += sentence;



  118
  —
  
    } else {



  119
  —
  
      if (current) {



  120
  —
  
        chunks.push({ content: current.trim(), charOffset: 0 });



  121
  —
  
      }



  122
  —
  
      // Start new chunk with overlap from previous



  123
  —
  
      const overlap = current.slice(-overlapChars);



  124
  —
  
      current = overlap + sentence;



  125
  —
  
    }



  126
  —
  
  }



  127
  
  



  128
  —
  
  if (current.trim()) {



  129
  —
  
    chunks.push({ content: current.trim(), charOffset: 0 });



  130
  —
  
  }



  131
  
  



  132
  —
  
  return chunks;



  133
  
  
}



  134

// inference.js — Unified inference facade



  2
  
  
//



  3
  
  
// Abstracts the WASM (Web Worker) vs. WebGPU (main thread) branching.



  4
  
  
// All consumers import from this single module; the facade routes calls



  5
  
  
// to the correct backend based on the current hardware.device setting.



  6
  
  
//



  7
  
  
// When device === 'wasm'   → delegates to wasmWorkerProxy (Web Worker)



  8
  
  
// When device === 'webgpu' → delegates to embedding.js + llm.js (main thread)



  9
  
  



  10
  
  
import { getState } from "./state.js";



  11
  
  



  12
  
  
// ── Lazy-loaded backends ──────────────────────────────────────────────



  13
  
  
// Each backend is loaded only when first needed for its device type.



  14
  
  



  15
  49
  
let _webgpuEmbedding = null;



  16
  49
  
let _webgpuLlm = null;



  17
  49
  
let _wasmProxy = null;



  18
  
  



  19
  
  
async function getWebgpuEmbedding() {



  20
  —
  
  if (!_webgpuEmbedding) {



  21
  —
  
    _webgpuEmbedding = await import("./embedding.js");



  22
  —
  
  }



  23
  —
  
  return _webgpuEmbedding;



  24
  
  
}



  25
  
  



  26
  
  
async function getWebgpuLlm() {



  27
  —
  
  if (!_webgpuLlm) {



  28
  —
  
    _webgpuLlm = await import("./llm.js");



  29
  —
  
  }



  30
  —
  
  return _webgpuLlm;



  31
  
  
}



  32
  
  



  33
  
  
async function getWasmProxy() {



  34
  —
  
  if (!_wasmProxy) {



  35
  —
  
    _wasmProxy = await import("./wasmWorkerProxy.js");



  36
  —
  
  }



  37
  —
  
  return _wasmProxy;



  38
  
  
}



  39
  
  



  40
  
  
// ── Facade-level state tracking ───────────────────────────────────────



  41
  
  
// Because `isEmbeddingLoaded()` and `isLLMLoaded()` are synchronous and



  42
  
  
// backends may not be loaded yet, we track readiness at the facade level.



  43
  
  
// All load/unload operations update this state.



  44
  
  



  45
  49
  
let _embeddingReady = false;



  46
  49
  
let _llmReady = false;



  47
  
  



  48
  
  
/** Return true when the current hardware config uses WASM. */



  49
  
  
function isWasm() {



  50
  —
  
  return getState().hardware.device === "wasm";



  51
  
  
}



  52
  
  



  53
  
  
// ── Embedding API ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────



  54
  
  



  55
  
  
/**



  56
  
  
 * Load the embedding model. Routes to Web Worker (WASM) or main thread (WebGPU).



  57
  
  
 * @param {Object} config - Hardware config with embeddingDtype and device



  58
  
  
 * @param {Function} [onProgress] - Optional progress callback



  59
  
  
 */



  60
  
  
export async function loadEmbeddingModel(config, onProgress) {



  61
  —
  
  _embeddingReady = false;



  62
  —
  
  try {



  63
  —
  
    if (isWasm()) {



  64
  —
  
      const mod = await getWasmProxy();



  65
  —
  
      await mod.loadEmbeddingModel(config, onProgress);



  66
  —
  
    } else {



  67
  —
  
      const mod = await getWebgpuEmbedding();



  68
  —
  
      await mod.loadEmbeddingModel(config, onProgress);



  69
  —
  
    }



  70
  —
  
    _embeddingReady = true;



  71
  —
  
  } catch (error) {



  72
  —
  
    _embeddingReady = false;



  73
  —
  
    throw error;



  74
  —
  
  }



  75
  
  
}



  76
  
  



  77
  
  
/**



  78
  
  
 * Embed a single user query with instruction wrapping.



  79
  
  
 * @param {string} query



  80
  
  
 * @returns {Promise<number[]>} 1024-dim embedding vector



  81
  
  
 */



  82
  
  
export async function embedQuery(query) {



  83
  —
  
  const mod = isWasm() ? await getWasmProxy() : await getWebgpuEmbedding();



  84
  —
  
  return mod.embedQuery(query);



  85
  
  
}



  86
  
  



  87
  
  
/**



  88
  
  
 * Embed multiple document texts (without instruction wrapping).



  89
  
  
 * @param {string[]} texts



  90
  
  
 * @returns {Promise<number[]>} Flat array of embedding data



  91
  
  
 */



  92
  
  
export async function embedDocuments(texts) {



  93
  —
  
  const mod = isWasm() ? await getWasmProxy() : await getWebgpuEmbedding();



  94
  —
  
  return mod.embedDocuments(texts);



  95
  
  
}



  96
  
  



  97
  
  
/**



  98
  
  
 * Convert flat batch embedding data into individual vectors.



  99
  
  
 * Pure CPU operation — no backend delegation needed.



  100
  
  
 * @param {number[]} flatData



  101
  
  
 * @param {number} batchSize



  102
  
  
 * @returns {number[][]}



  103
  
  
 */



  104
  
  
export function getEmbeddingArrays(flatData, batchSize) {



  105
  —
  
  const dim = flatData.length / batchSize;



  106
  —
  
  const arrays = [];



  107
  —
  
  for (let i = 0; i < batchSize; i++) {



  108
  —
  
    const start = i * dim;



  109
  —
  
    arrays.push(flatData.slice(start, start + dim));



  110
  —
  
  }



  111
  —
  
  return arrays;



  112
  
  
}



  113
  
  



  114
  
  
/**



  115
  
  
 * Dispose the embedding model and free memory.



  116
  
  
 */



  117
  
  
export async function unloadEmbeddingModel() {



  118
  —
  
  if (_embeddingReady) {



  119
  —
  
    if (isWasm()) {



  120
  —
  
      const mod = await getWasmProxy();



  121
  —
  
      await mod.unloadEmbeddingModel();



  122
  —
  
    } else {



  123
  —
  
      const mod = await getWebgpuEmbedding();



  124
  —
  
      await mod.unloadEmbeddingModel();



  125
  —
  
    }



  126
  —
  
  }



  127
  —
  
  _embeddingReady = false;



  128
  
  
}



  129
  
  



  130
  
  
/**



  131
  
  
 * Check if the embedding model is currently loaded.



  132
  
  
 */



  133
  
  
export function isEmbeddingLoaded() {



  134
  —
  
  return _embeddingReady;



  135
  
  
}



  136

// ── LLM API ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────



  138
  
  



  139
  
  
/**



  140
  
  
 * Load the LLM. Routes to Web Worker (WASM) or main thread (WebGPU).



  141
  
  
 * @param {Object} config - Hardware config with llmModelId, llmDtype and device



  142
  
  
 * @param {Function} [onProgress] - Optional progress callback



  143
  
  
 */



  144
  
  
export async function loadLLM(config, onProgress) {



  145
  —
  
  _llmReady = false;



  146
  —
  
  try {



  147
  —
  
    if (isWasm()) {



  148
  —
  
      const mod = await getWasmProxy();



  149
  —
  
      await mod.loadLLM(config, onProgress);



  150
  —
  
    } else {



  151
  —
  
      const mod = await getWebgpuLlm();



  152
  —
  
      await mod.loadLLM(config, onProgress);



  153
  —
  
    }



  154
  —
  
    _llmReady = true;



  155
  —
  
  } catch (error) {



  156
  —
  
    _llmReady = false;



  157
  —
  
    throw error;



  158
  —
  
  }



  159
  
  
}



  160
  
  



  161
  
  
/**



  162
  
  
 * Generate a response from the LLM.



  163
  
  
 * @param {Array} messages - Conversation messages



  164
  
  
 * @param {Function} onToken - Streaming callback



  165
  
  
 * @param {Function} onComplete - Completion callback



  166
  
  
 * @param {Function} [onPhase] - Phase update callback



  167
  
  
 * @returns {Promise<{text: string, outputTokenCount: number}>}



  168
  
  
 */



  169
  
  
export async function generateResponse(messages, onToken, onComplete, onPhase) {



  170
  —
  
  const mod = isWasm() ? await getWasmProxy() : await getWebgpuLlm();



  171
  —
  
  return mod.generateResponse(messages, onToken, onComplete, onPhase);



  172
  
  
}



  173
  
  



  174
  
  
/**



  175
  
  
 * Stop the current generation (if one is in progress).



  176
  
  
 */



  177
  
  
export function stopGeneration() {



  178
  —
  
  if (isWasm()) {



  179
  —
  
    // WASM proxy stop is async (message to worker) — fire and forget



  180
  —
  
    if (_wasmProxy) {



  181
  —
  
      _wasmProxy.stopGeneration().catch(() => {});



  182
  —
  
    }



  183
  —
  
  } else {



  184
  —
  
    // WebGPU stop is synchronous



  185
  —
  
    if (_webgpuLlm) {



  186
  —
  
      _webgpuLlm.stopGeneration();



  187
  —
  
    }



  188
  —
  
  }



  189
  
  
}



  190
  
  



  191
  
  
/**



  192
  
  
 * Dispose the LLM model and free memory.



  193
  
  
 */



  194
  
  
export async function unloadLLM() {



  195
  —
  
  if (_llmReady) {



  196
  —
  
    if (isWasm()) {



  197
  —
  
      const mod = await getWasmProxy();



  198
  —
  
      await mod.unloadLLM();



  199
  —
  
    } else {



  200
  —
  
      const mod = await getWebgpuLlm();



  201
  —
  
      await mod.unloadLLM();



  202
  —
  
    }



  203
  —
  
  }



  204
  —
  
  _llmReady = false;



  205
  
  
}



  206
  
  



  207
  
  
/**



  208
  
  
 * Check if the LLM model is currently loaded.



  209
  
  
 */



  210
  
  
export function isLLMLoaded() {



  211
  —
  
  return _llmReady;



  212
  
  
}



  213
  
  



  214
  
  
/**



  215
  
  
 * Return the context window for the currently loaded model.



  216
  
  
 */



  217
  
  
export function getContextWindow() {



  218
  —
  
  if (_webgpuLlm) return _webgpuLlm.getContextWindow();



  219
  —
  
  if (_wasmProxy) return _wasmProxy.getContextWindow();



  220
  
  
  // Fallback defaults before any model is loaded



  221
  —
  
  return isWasm() ? 4096 : 32768;



  222
  
  
}



  223
  
  



  224
  
  
/**



  225
  
  
 * Check if the currently loaded model supports thinking mode.



  226
  
  
 * Both WebGPU (Qwen3.5) and WASM (Qwen3-0.6B) support thinking.



  227
  
  
 * WebGPU uses chat template options; WASM uses /think and /no_think suffixes.



  228
  
  
 */



  229
  
  
export function supportsThinking() {



  230
  —
  
  if (_webgpuLlm) return _webgpuLlm.supportsThinking();



  231
  —
  
  if (_wasmProxy) return _wasmProxy.supportsThinking();



  232
  
  
  // Default: both models support thinking until proven otherwise



  233
  —
  
  return true;



  234
  
  
}

// utils.js — Utility functions (UUID, token estimation, formatting, streaming diff)



  2
  
  



  3
  
  
/**



  4
  
  
 * Generate a unique UUID v4 using the browser's crypto API.



  5
  
  
 * Available in all modern browsers (requires secure context / HTTPS or localhost).



  6
  
  
 */



  7
  
  
export function generateUUID() {



  8
  —
  
  return crypto.randomUUID();



  9
  
  
}



  10
  
  



  11
  
  
/**



  12
  
  
 * Rough token estimation for English text.



  13
  
  
 * Uses the heuristic: ~4 characters per token.



  14
  
  
 * Not accurate for all languages or tokenizers, but sufficient for chunking bounds.



  15
  
  
 */



  16
  
  
export function estimateTokens(text) {



  17
  —
  
  return Math.ceil(text.length / 4);



  18
  
  
}



  19
  
  



  20
  
  
/**



  21
  
  
 * Format byte count into human-readable string (e.g. "1.5 MB").



  22
  
  
 */



  23
  
  
export function formatBytes(bytes) {



  24
  —
  
  if (bytes === 0) return "0 B";



  25
  —
  
  const k = 1024;



  26
  —
  
  const sizes = ["B", "KB", "MB", "GB"];



  27
  —
  
  const i = Math.floor(Math.log(bytes) / Math.log(k));



  28
  —
  
  return `${(bytes / Math.pow(k, i)).toFixed(1)} ${sizes[i]}`;



  29
  
  
}



  30
  
  



  31
  
  
/**



  32
  
  
 * Extract only the new text from accumulated text.



  33
  
  
 * The onToken callback receives the FULL accumulated text after generation



  34
  
  
 * completes, so we diff against the previous value to get the delta.



  35
  
  
 */



  36
  
  
export function getNewText(fullText, previousText) {



  37
  —
  
  return fullText.slice(previousText.length);



  38
  
  
}



  39
  
  



  40
  
  
// ─── Token Tracking Utilities ───────────────────────────────────────



  41
  
  



  42
  
  
/**



  43
  
  
 * Chat template adds formatting tokens (role markers, separators, special tokens).



  44
  
  
 * This multiplier accounts for the overhead the processor.apply_chat_template() adds.



  45
  
  
 */



  46
  49
  
const CHAT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD = 1.15;



  47
  
  



  48
  
  
/**



  49
  
  
 * Estimate tokens for an array of messages, accounting for chat template overhead.



  50
  
  
 * Each message is { role, content: [{ type: 'text', text: '...' }] } or { role, content: '...' }



  51
  
  
 */



  52
  
  
export function estimateInputTokens(messages) {



  53
  —
  
  if (!messages || messages.length === 0) return 0;



  54
  
  



  55
  —
  
  const rawCharCount = messages.reduce((sum, msg) => {



  56
  —
  
    const text =



  57
  —
  
      typeof msg.content === "string"



  58
  —
  
        ? msg.content



  59
  —
  
        : msg.content?.[0]?.type === "text"



  60
  —
  
          ? msg.content[0].text



  61
  —
  
          : "";



  62
  —
  
    return sum + text.length;



  63
  —
  
  }, 0);



  64
  
  



  65
  —
  
  return Math.ceil((rawCharCount / 4) * CHAT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD);



  66
  
  
}



  67
  
  



  68
  
  
/**



  69
  
  
 * Format token count with K/M suffix for display.



  70
  
  
 */



  71
  
  
export function formatTokens(n) {



  72
  —
  
  if (n >= 1_000_000) return `${(n / 1_000_000).toFixed(1)}M`;



  73
  62
  
  if (n >= 1_000) return `${(n / 1_000).toFixed(1)}K`;



  74
  31
  
  return n.toString();



  75
  
  
}



  76
  
  



  77
  
  
/**



  78
  
  
 * Determine warning level based on context window usage percentage.



  79
  
  
 * The banner only appears once usage exceeds 75% of the context window.



  80
  
  
 *



  81
  
  
 * Levels:



  82
  
  
 *   ok        — usage ≤ 75% (no banner)



  83
  
  
 *   caution   — usage > 75% (gentle suggestion)



  84
  
  
 *   warning   — usage > 90% (stronger message)



  85
  
  
 *   critical  — usage > 95% (urgent)



  86
  
  
 *   exceeded  — remaining ≤ 0 (already full)



  87
  
  
 *



  88
  
  
 * @param {number} remaining - Remaining tokens in context window



  89
  
  
 * @param {number} contextWindow - Total context window size



  90
  
  
 * @returns {'ok' | 'caution' | 'warning' | 'critical' | 'exceeded'}



  91
  
  
 */



  92
  
  
export function getWarningLevel(remaining, contextWindow) {



  93
  —
  
  if (remaining <= 0) return "exceeded";



  94
  —
  
  const usage = (contextWindow - remaining) / contextWindow;



  95
  —
  
  if (usage > 0.95) return "critical";



  96
  —
  
  if (usage > 0.9) return "warning";



  97
  —
  
  if (usage > 0.75) return "caution";



  98
  —
  
  return "ok";



  99
  
  
}



  100
  
  



  101
  
  
// ─── Thinking Tag Utilities ───────────────────────────────────────────



  102
  
  



  103
  
  
/**



  104
  
  
 * Ensure thinking content is properly wrapped in `` tags.



  105
  
  
 *



  106
  
  
 * When a chat template places the opening `` tag in the prompt



  107
  
  
 * (input tokens), the model generates only the closing `` tag.



  108
  
  
 * This function detects that case and wraps the pre-close-tag content



  109
  
  
 * with the opening tag so that renderer.preprocessThinking() can



  110
  
  
 * convert it into a collapsible `<details>` element.



  111
  
  
 *



  112
  
  
 * @param {string} content - Raw model output



  113
  
  
 * @param {boolean} enableThinking - Whether thinking mode is active



  114
  
  
 * @returns {string} Content with proper think tags



  115
  
  
 */



  116
  
  
export function ensureThinkTags(content, enableThinking) {



  117
  —
  
  if (!enableThinking || !content) return content;



  118
  —
  
  const openTag = "<" + "think>";



  119
  —
  
  const closeTag = "<" + "/think>";



  120
  —
  
  const closeIndex = content.indexOf(closeTag);



  121
  
  
  // Only fix if closing tag exists but opening tag is missing



  122
  —
  
  if (closeIndex !== -1 && !content.startsWith(openTag)) {



  123
  —
  
    const thinkingContent = content.substring(0, closeIndex).trim();



  124
  —
  
    const answerContent = content.substring(closeIndex + closeTag.length);



  125
  —
  
    return openTag + "\n" + thinkingContent + "\n" + closeTag + answerContent;



  126
  —
  
  }



  127
  —
  
  return content;



  128
  
  
}



  129
  
  



      
    

  


  

    
      /home/robert/AI_Projects/RAG-Browser/rag-v2-qwen3.6-27b/js/llm.js
      
        10.1%
        0.0%
        0.0%
        2.9%
      
    
    

      

  1
  
  
// llm.js — LLM model loader, generation (batch decode), and disposal



  2
  
  



  3
  
  
import {



  4
  
  
  AutoProcessor,



  5
  
  
  Qwen3_5ForConditionalGeneration,



  6
  
  
  InterruptableStoppingCriteria,



  7
  
  
  pipeline,



  8
  
  
} from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@huggingface/transformers@4.2.0/+esm";



  9
  
  



  10
  
  
import { getState } from "./state.js";



  11
  
  
import { ensureThinkTags } from "./utils.js";



  12
  
  



  13
  
  
// ─── Model-specific constants ──────────────────────────────────────────



  14
  
  



  15
  49
  
const LLM_MODEL_WEBGPU = "huggingworld/Qwen3.5-2B-ONNX";



  16
  
  



  17
  
  
// Qwen3.5-2B (WebGPU): 32K practical context in browser



  18
  49
  
const CONTEXT_WINDOW_WEBGPU = 32768;



  19
  
  
// Qwen3-0.6B-Instruct (WASM): 4K native context



  20
  49
  
const CONTEXT_WINDOW_WASM = 4096;



  21
  
  



  22
  
  
// WASM pipeline (Qwen3-0.6B-Instruct) token cap.



  23
  
  
// q4 WASM does ~2-5 tok/s; 4096 tokens ≈ 14-36 min worst-case.



  24
  
  
// WebGPU is uncapped (8192) since it's GPU-accelerated.



  25
  49
  
const WASM_MAX_NEW_TOKENS = 4096;



  26
  
  



  27
  
  
// Maximum thinking tokens allowed for WASM inference.



  28
  
  
// The pipeline API does not support max_thinking_tokens, so this



  29
  
  
// value is fixed and the corresponding UI slider is frozen.



  30
  49
  
const WASM_MAX_THINKING_TOKENS = 1024;



  31
  
  



  32
  
  
// ─── Module state ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────



  33
  
  



  34
  49
  
let processor = null;



  35
  49
  
let model = null; // Qwen3.5 low-level model



  36
  49
  
let generator = null; // Pipeline generator for Qwen3-0.6B-Instruct



  37
  49
  
let currentStoppingCriteria = null;



  38
  49
  
let modelType = null; // 'qwen3_5' | 'pipeline'



  39
  
  



  40
  
  
/**



  41
  
  
 * Return the context window for the currently loaded model.



  42
  
  
 */



  43
  
  
export function getContextWindow() {



  44
  —
  
  if (modelType === "qwen3_5") return CONTEXT_WINDOW_WEBGPU;



  45
  —
  
  return CONTEXT_WINDOW_WASM;



  46
  
  
}



  47
  
  



  48
  
  
// Export WASM limits so the UI can enforce them on sliders.



  49
  
  
export { WASM_MAX_NEW_TOKENS, WASM_MAX_THINKING_TOKENS };



  50
  
  



  51
  
  
/**



  52
  
  
 * Check if the currently loaded model supports thinking mode.



  53
  
  
 */



  54
  
  
export function supportsThinking() {



  55
  —
  
  return modelType === "qwen3_5";



  56
  
  
}



  57
  
  



  58
  
  
/**



  59
  
  
 * Load the LLM processor and model.



  60
  
  
 *



  61
  
  
 * WebGPU  → Qwen3.5-2B: low-level API (multi-modal architecture)



  62
  
  
 * WASM    → Qwen3-0.6B-Instruct: pipeline API (instruction-tuned causal LM)



  63
  
  
 *



  64
  
  
 * @param {Object} config - Hardware config with llmModelId, llmDtype and device



  65
  
  
 * @param {Function} [onProgress] - Optional progress callback from transformers.js



  66
  
  
 */



  67
  
  
export async function loadLLM(config, onProgress) {



  68
  —
  
  if (config.llmModelId === LLM_MODEL_WEBGPU) {



  69
  —
  
    // ── Qwen3.5-2B (WebGPU) — low-level API ──────────────────────────



  70
  —
  
    // Uses AutoProcessor (multi-modal) + Qwen3_5ForConditionalGeneration.



  71
  —
  
    // The pipeline() API does NOT correctly handle this architecture.



  72
  —
  
    modelType = "qwen3_5";



  73
  —
  
    processor = await AutoProcessor.from_pretrained(config.llmModelId);



  74
  —
  
    model = await Qwen3_5ForConditionalGeneration.from_pretrained(



  75
  —
  
      config.llmModelId,



  76
  —
  
      {



  77
  —
  
        dtype: config.llmDtype,



  78
  —
  
        device: config.device,



  79
  —
  
        progress_callback: onProgress,



  80
  —
  
      },



  81
  —
  
    );



  82
  —
  
  } else {



  83
  —
  
    // ── Qwen3-0.6B-Instruct (WASM) — pipeline API ─────────────────────────



  84
  —
  
    // Instruction-tuned decoder-only model. pipeline() handles tokenization,



  85
  —
  
    // chat templating, and generation automatically.



  86
  —
  
    modelType = "pipeline";



  87
  —
  
    generator = await pipeline("text-generation", config.llmModelId, {



  88
  —
  
      dtype: config.llmDtype,



  89
  —
  
      device: config.device,



  90
  —
  
      progress_callback: onProgress,



  91
  —
  
    });



  92
  —
  
  }



  93
  
  
}



  94
  
  



  95
  
  
// ─── Generation ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────



  96
  
  



  97
  
  
/**



  98
  
  
 * Generate a response from the LLM.



  99
  
  
 *



  100
  
  
 * Messages are expected in the nested content format:



  101
  
  
 *   [{ role: 'system', content: [{ type: 'text', text: '...' }] }, ...]



  102
  
  
 *



  103
  
  
 * For Qwen3.5 (WebGPU): applies chat template + model.generate() + batch decode.



  104
  
  
 * For Qwen3-0.6B-Instruct (WASM): converts to flat messages + pipeline call.



  105
  
  
 *



  106
  
  
 * @param {Array} messages - Conversation messages (nested content format)



  107
  
  
 * @param {Function} onToken - Callback receiving final full text (called once after generation)



  108
  
  
 * @param {Function} onComplete - Callback receiving final full response text



  109
  
  
 * @param {Function} [onPhase] - Optional callback for phase updates during generation



  110
  
  
 * @returns {Promise<string>} The full generated response text



  111
  
  
 */



  112
  
  
export async function generateResponse(messages, onToken, onComplete, onPhase) {



  113
  
  
  // Create interruptible stopping criteria for stop-generation support



  114
  —
  
  currentStoppingCriteria = new InterruptableStoppingCriteria();



  115
  
  



  116
  
  
  const {



  117
  
  
    llmConfig: { generation },



  118
  —
  
  } = getState();



  119
  
  



  120
  —
  
  if (modelType === "qwen3_5") {



  121
  —
  
    return generateQwen3_5(messages, onToken, onComplete, onPhase, generation);



  122
  —
  
  }



  123
  
  



  124
  
  
  // modelType === "pipeline"



  125
  —
  
  return generatePipeline(messages, onToken, onComplete, onPhase, generation);



  126
  
  
}



  127
  
  



  128
  
  
/**



  129
  
  
 * Generate with Qwen3.5-2B (low-level API).



  130
  
  
 * Collects all tokens then batch-decodes — avoids TextStreamer incremental



  131
  
  
 * BPE decoding bug that corrupts Qwen3.5 output.



  132
  
  
 */



  133
  
  
async function generateQwen3_5(



  134
  
  
  messages,



  135
  
  
  onToken,



  136
  
  
  onComplete,



  137
  
  
  onPhase,



  138
  
  
  generation,



  139
  
  
) {



  140
  
  
  // Transform to flat format for processor.apply_chat_template()



  141
  —
  
  const formattedMessages = messages.map((msg) => ({



  142
  —
  
    role: msg.role,



  143
  —
  
    content:



  144
  —
  
      typeof msg.content === "string"



  145
  —
  
        ? msg.content



  146
  —
  
        : msg.content?.[0]?.type === "text"



  147
  —
  
          ? msg.content[0].text



  148
  —
  
          : "",



  149
  —
  
  }));



  150
  
  



  151
  
  
  // Apply chat template



  152
  —
  
  const { llmConfig } = getState();



  153
  —
  
  const templateOptions = {



  154
  —
  
    add_generation_prompt: true,



  155
  —
  
    enable_thinking: llmConfig.enableThinking,



  156
  —
  
  };



  157
  —
  
  if (llmConfig.enableThinking) {



  158
  —
  
    templateOptions.max_thinking_tokens = llmConfig.maxThinkingTokens;



  159
  —
  
  }



  160
  —
  
  const text = processor.apply_chat_template(



  161
  —
  
    formattedMessages,



  162
  —
  
    templateOptions,



  163
  —
  
  );



  164
  
  



  165
  
  
  // Tokenize



  166
  —
  
  const inputs = await processor(text);



  167
  —
  
  const inputLength = inputs.input_ids.dims[1];



  168
  
  



  169
  
  
  // Phase tracking



  170
  —
  
  let genInterval = null;



  171
  —
  
  if (onPhase) {



  172
  —
  
    const initialPhase = llmConfig.enableThinking



  173
  —
  
      ? "generating_thinking"



  174
  —
  
      : "generating";



  175
  —
  
    onPhase(initialPhase);



  176
  —
  



  177
  —
  
    const startTime = Date.now();



  178
  —
  
    let currentPhase = initialPhase;



  179
  —
  



  180
  —
  
    genInterval = setInterval(() => {



  181
  —
  
      const elapsed = (Date.now() - startTime) / 1000;



  182
  —
  
      let newPhase = currentPhase;



  183
  —
  



  184
  —
  
      if (llmConfig.enableThinking) {



  185
  —
  
        if (elapsed >= 50) newPhase = "generating_finalizing";



  186
  —
  
        else if (elapsed >= 20) newPhase = "generating_formulating";



  187
  —
  
      } else {



  188
  —
  
        if (elapsed >= 30) newPhase = "generating_finalizing";



  189
  —
  
        else if (elapsed >= 10) newPhase = "generating_composing";



  190
  —
  
      }



  191
  —
  



  192
  —
  
      if (newPhase !== currentPhase) {



  193
  —
  
        currentPhase = newPhase;



  194
  —
  
        onPhase(newPhase);



  195
  —
  
      }



  196
  —
  
    }, 2000);



  197
  —
  
  }



  198
  
  



  199
  —
  
  try {



  200
  —
  
    const output = await model.generate({



  201
  —
  
      ...inputs,



  202
  —
  
      max_new_tokens: generation.max_new_tokens,



  203
  —
  
      do_sample: true,



  204
  —
  
      temperature: generation.temperature,



  205
  —
  
      top_p: generation.top_p,



  206
  —
  
      top_k: generation.top_k,



  207
  —
  
      min_p: generation.min_p,



  208
  —
  
      presence_penalty: generation.presence_penalty,



  209
  —
  
      repetition_penalty: generation.repetition_penalty,



  210
  —
  
      stopping_criteria: [currentStoppingCriteria],



  211
  —
  
    });



  212
  —
  



  213
  —
  
    // Batch decode all generated tokens



  214
  —
  
    const rawOutput = Array.from(output.data);



  215
  —
  
    const generatedTokenIds = rawOutput.slice(inputLength);



  216
  —
  
    let fullText = processor.tokenizer.decode(generatedTokenIds, {



  217
  —
  
      skip_special_tokens: true,



  218
  —
  
    });



  219
  —
  



  220
  —
  
    const outputTokenCount = generatedTokenIds.length;



  221
  —
  



  222
  —
  
    // Ensure thinking content is wrapped in proper &lt;think&gt; tags.



  223
  —
  
    // The chat template places the opening tag in the prompt, so the model



  224
  —
  
    // generates only &lt;/think&gt;. ensureThinkTags wraps the content properly.



  225
  —
  
    fullText = ensureThinkTags(fullText, llmConfig.enableThinking);



  226
  —
  



  227
  —
  
    if (onToken) onToken(fullText);



  228
  —
  
    if (onComplete) onComplete(fullText, { outputTokenCount });



  229
  —
  



  230
  —
  
    currentStoppingCriteria = null;



  231
  —
  
    return { text: fullText, outputTokenCount };



  232
  —
  
  } finally {



  233
  —
  
    if (genInterval) clearInterval(genInterval);



  234
  —
  
  }



  235
  
  
}



  236
  
  



  237
  
  
/**



  238
  
  
 * Generate with Qwen3-0.6B-Instruct via pipeline API.



  239
  
  
 * Converts nested content messages to flat format expected by the pipeline.



  240
  
  
 */



  241
  
  
async function generatePipeline(



  242
  
  
  messages,



  243
  
  
  onToken,



  244
  
  
  onComplete,



  245
  
  
  onPhase,



  246
  
  
  generation,



  247
  
  
) {



  248
  
  
  // Read config early for phase tracking and post-processing



  249
  —
  
  const { llmConfig: pipelineLlmConfig } = getState();



  250
  
  



  251
  
  
  // Convert nested content messages to flat format for pipeline



  252
  —
  
  const flatMessages = messages.map((msg) => ({



  253
  —
  
    role: msg.role,



  254
  —
  
    content:



  255
  —
  
      typeof msg.content === "string"



  256
  —
  
        ? msg.content



  257
  —
  
        : msg.content?.[0]?.type === "text"



  258
  —
  
          ? msg.content[0].text



  259
  —
  
          : "",



  260
  —
  
  }));



  261
  
  



  262
  
  
  // Phase tracking



  263
  —
  
  if (onPhase) {



  264
  —
  
    onPhase(



  265
  —
  
      pipelineLlmConfig.enableThinking ? "generating_thinking" : "generating",



  266
  —
  
    );



  267
  —
  
  }



  268
  
  



  269
  
  
  // Generate via pipeline (no streaming — full output at once)



  270
  —
  
  const cappedTokens = Math.min(generation.max_new_tokens, WASM_MAX_NEW_TOKENS);



  271
  —
  
  const output = await generator(flatMessages, {



  272
  —
  
    max_new_tokens: cappedTokens,



  273
  —
  
    do_sample: true,



  274
  —
  
    temperature: generation.temperature,



  275
  —
  
    top_p: generation.top_p,



  276
  —
  
    top_k: generation.top_k,



  277
  —
  
    repetition_penalty: generation.repetition_penalty,



  278
  —
  
    return_full_text: false,



  279
  —
  
    stopping_criteria: [currentStoppingCriteria],



  280
  —
  
  });



  281
  
  



  282
  
  
  // Pipeline returns: [{ generated_text: [...messages including assistant response] }]



  283
  
  
  // Extract the assistant's response from the last generated message



  284
  —
  
  const generatedMessages = output[0].generated_text;



  285
  —
  
  const assistantMessage = generatedMessages[generatedMessages.length - 1];



  286
  
  
  const fullText =



  287
  —
  
    typeof assistantMessage.content === "string"



  288
  —
  
      ? assistantMessage.content



  289
  —
  
      : "";



  290
  
  



  291
  
  
  // Estimate output tokens from text length (~4 chars per token)



  292
  —
  
  const outputTokenCount = Math.ceil(fullText.length / 4);



  293
  
  



  294
  
  
  // Ensure thinking content is wrapped in proper think tags



  295
  —
  
  const processedText = ensureThinkTags(



  296
  —
  
    fullText,



  297
  —
  
    pipelineLlmConfig.enableThinking,



  298
  —
  
  );



  299
  
  



  300
  —
  
  if (onToken) onToken(processedText);



  301
  —
  
  if (onComplete) onComplete(processedText, { outputTokenCount });



  302
  
  



  303
  —
  
  currentStoppingCriteria = null;



  304
  —
  
  return { text: processedText, outputTokenCount };



  305
  
  
}



  306
  
  



  307
  
  
/**



  308
  
  
 * Stop the current generation (if one is in progress).



  309
  
  
 * Uses InterruptableStoppingCriteria.interrupt() which stops at the next checkpoint.



  310
  
  
 */



  311
  
  
export function stopGeneration() {



  312
  —
  
  if (currentStoppingCriteria) {



  313
  —
  
    currentStoppingCriteria.interrupt();



  314
  —
  
  }



  315
  
  
}



  316
  
  



  317
  
  
/**



  318
  
  
 * Dispose the LLM model, freeing memory.



  319
  
  
 */



  320
  
  
export async function unloadLLM() {



  321
  —
  
  if (model) {



  322
  —
  
    await model.dispose();



  323
  —
  
    model = null;



  324
  —
  
  }



  325
  —
  
  if (generator) {



  326
  —
  
    await generator.dispose();



  327
  —
  
    generator = null;



  328
  —
  
  }



  329
  —
  
  modelType = null;



  330
  —
  
  processor = null;



  331
  
  
}



  332
  
  



  333
  
  
/**



  334
  
  
 * Check if the LLM model is currently loaded.



  335
  
  
 */



  336
  
  
export function isLLMLoaded() {



  337
  —
  
  return (model !== null || generator !== null) && modelType !== null;



  338
  
  
}



  339
  
  



      
    

  


  

    
      /home/robert/AI_Projects/RAG-Browser/rag-v2-qwen3.6-27b/js/rag-pipeline.js

// rag-pipeline.js — RAG pipeline orchestration (ingestion + retrieval & generation)



  2
  
  



  3
  
  
import { chunkText } from "./chunker.js";



  4
  
  
import {



  5
  
  
  embedQuery,



  6
  
  
  embedDocuments,



  7
  
  
  getEmbeddingArrays,



  8
  
  
  generateResponse,



  9
  
  
} from "./inference.js";



  10
  
  
import { parseFile, needsOfficeParser, needsPdfJs } from "./fileParser.js";



  11
  
  
import { insertChunks, searchHybrid, searchVector } from "./orama-db.js";



  12
  
  
import { getRecentHistory, getState, updateTokenTracking } from "./state.js";



  13
  
  
import { estimateInputTokens } from "./utils.js";



  14
  
  



  15
  
  
// ─── Ingestion Pipeline ───────────────────────────────────────────────



  16
  
  



  17
  
  
/**



  18
  
  
 * Ingest a supported document: parse, chunk, embed, and index in Orama.



  19
  
  
 * Supports: .txt, .md, .csv, .xls, .xlsx, .docx, .pptx, .odt, .ods, .odp, .pdf



  20
  
  
 *



  21
  
  
 * @param {File} file - The uploaded file



  22
  
  
 * @param {Object} db - Orama database instance



  23
  
  
 * @param {Function} progressCallback - Called with { step, progress, message } at each stage



  24
  
  
 * @param {Object} options - Optional ingestion options



  25
  
  
 * @param {number} options.maxTokens - Max tokens per chunk (default 512)



  26
  
  
 * @returns {Promise<{ chunks: number, fileSize: number }>}



  27
  
  
 */



  28
  
  
export async function ingestDocument(file, db, progressCallback, options = {}) {



  29
  —
  
  const { maxTokens = 512 } = options;



  30
  
  
  // Step 1: Read/parse file



  31
  —
  
  progressCallback({



  32
  —
  
    step: "reading",



  33
  —
  
    progress: 0,



  34
  —
  
    message: `Reading ${file.name}...`,



  35
  —
  
  });



  36
  —
  
  const fileSize = file.size;



  37
  
  



  38
  
  
  // For office files (docx, xlsx, etc.), load parser first



  39
  —
  
  if (needsOfficeParser(file.name)) {



  40
  —
  
    progressCallback({



  41
  —
  
      step: "loading-parser",



  42
  —
  
      progress: 5,



  43
  —
  
      message: `Loading parser for ${file.name}...`,



  44
  —
  
    });



  45
  —
  
  }



  46
  
  



  47
  
  
  // For PDF files, load PDF.js first



  48
  —
  
  if (needsPdfJs(file.name)) {



  49
  —
  
    progressCallback({



  50
  —
  
      step: "loading-pdf-parser",



  51
  —
  
      progress: 5,



  52
  —
  
      message: `Loading PDF parser for ${file.name}...`,



  53
  —
  
    });



  54
  —
  
  }



  55
  
  



  56
  —
  
  const text = await parseFile(file);



  57
  
  



  58
  
  
  // Step 2: Chunk text



  59
  —
  
  progressCallback({



  60
  —
  
    step: "chunking",



  61
  —
  
    progress: 25,



  62
  —
  
    message: "Chunking document...",



  63
  —
  
  });



  64
  —
  
  const chunks = chunkText(text, { sourceFile: file.name, maxTokens });



  65
  
  



  66
  
  
  // Step 3: Generate embeddings (in batches)



  67
  —
  
  progressCallback({



  68
  —
  
    step: "embedding",



  69
  —
  
    progress: 50,



  70
  —
  
    message: `Generating embeddings for ${chunks.length} chunks...`,



  71
  —
  
  });



  72
  —
  
  const batchSize = 32;



  73
  —
  
  const allEmbeddings = [];



  74
  
  



  75
  —
  
  for (let i = 0; i < chunks.length; i += batchSize) {



  76
  —
  
    const batch = chunks.slice(i, i + batchSize).map((c) => c.content);



  77
  —
  
    const flatData = await embedDocuments(batch);



  78
  —
  
    const embeddingArrays = getEmbeddingArrays(flatData, batch.length);



  79
  —
  
    allEmbeddings.push(...embeddingArrays);



  80
  —
  



  81
  —
  
    const embedProgress =



  82
  —
  
      50 + (Math.min(i + batchSize, chunks.length) / chunks.length) * 40;



  83
  —
  
    progressCallback({



  84
  —
  
      step: "embedding",



  85
  —
  
      progress: embedProgress,



  86
  —
  
      message: `Embedded ${Math.min(i + batchSize, chunks.length)}/${chunks.length} chunks...`,



  87
  —
  
    });



  88
  —
  
  }



  89
  
  



  90
  
  
  // Step 4: Insert into Orama



  91
  —
  
  progressCallback({



  92
  —
  
    step: "indexing",



  93
  —
  
    progress: 95,



  94
  —
  
    message: "Indexing chunks...",



  95
  —
  
  });



  96
  —
  
  const documents = chunks.map((chunk, i) => ({



  97
  —
  
    ...chunk,



  98
  —
  
    embedding: allEmbeddings[i],



  99
  —
  
  }));



  100
  
  



  101
  —
  
  await insertChunks(db, documents);



  102
  
  



  103
  —
  
  progressCallback({



  104
  —
  
    step: "complete",



  105
  —
  
    progress: 100,



  106
  —
  
    message: `Indexed ${chunks.length} chunks from ${file.name}`,



  107
  —
  
  });



  108
  
  



  109
  —
  
  return { chunks: chunks.length, fileSize };



  110
  
  
}



  111
  
  



  112
  
  
// ─── Retrieval & Generation Pipeline ──────────────────────────────────



  113
  
  



  114
  
  
/**



  115
  
  
 * System prompt template for RAG generation (non-thinking mode).



  116
  
  
 * Context and question are injected at runtime.



  117
  
  
 */



  118
  49
  
const SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are a helpful assistant. Answer the user's question using ONLY the provided context. If the context does not contain enough information, say so clearly. Cite your sources by referencing the document chunks.



  119
  49
  



  120
  49
  
Context:



  121
  49
  
{context}



  122
  49
  



  123
  49
  
Question: {question}`;



  124
  
  



  125
  
  
/**



  126
  
  
 * System prompt template for RAG generation (thinking mode).



  127
  
  
 * Instructs Qwen3.5 to keep reasoning brief and focused, avoiding



  128
  
  
 * over-analysis. Context and question are injected at runtime.



  129
  
  
 */



  130
  49
  
const SYSTEM_PROMPT_THINKING = `You are a helpful assistant. Keep your reasoning brief and focused. Do not loop. Do NOT overthink. Answer the user's question using ONLY the provided context and highlight the final conclusion. If the context does not contain enough information, say so clearly. Cite your sources by referencing the document chunks.



  131
  49
  



  132
  49
  
Context:



  133
  49
  
{context}



  134
  49
  



  135
  49
  
Question: {question}`;



  136
  
  



  137
  
  
/**



  138
  
  
 * Maximum number of conversation history messages to include (10 = 5 exchanges).



  139
  
  
 */



  140
  49
  
const MAX_HISTORY = 10;



  141
  
  



  142
  
  
/**



  143
  
  
 * Retrieve relevant chunks and generate a streaming response.



  144
  
  
 *



  145
  
  
 * @param {string} query - User's question



  146
  
  
 * @param {Object} db - Orama database instance



  147
  
  
 * @param {Function} onToken - Streaming callback receiving accumulated text



  148
  
  
 * @param {Function} onComplete - Completion callback receiving final text



  149
  
  
 * @param {Function} [onPhase] - Optional callback for UI phase updates ("embedding", "searching", "generating")



  150
  
  
 * @returns {Promise<{ sourceChunks: Array, similarity: Array }>}



  151
  
  
 */



  152
  
  
export async function retrieveAndGenerate(



  153
  
  
  query,



  154
  
  
  db,



  155
  
  
  onToken,



  156
  
  
  onComplete,



  157
  
  
  onPhase,



  158
  
  
) {



  159
  
  
  // Step 1: Embed query (with instruction wrapper)



  160
  —
  
  if (onPhase) onPhase("embedding");



  161
  —
  
  const queryEmbedding = await embedQuery(query);



  162
  
  



  163
  
  
  // Step 2: Get current configuration from state



  164
  —
  
  const { searchConfig, llmConfig } = getState();



  165
  —
  
  const enableThinking = llmConfig.enableThinking;



  166
  
  



  167
  
  
  // Step 3: Search for relevant chunks



  168
  —
  
  if (onPhase) onPhase("searching");



  169
  
  
  let results;



  170
  —
  
  if (searchConfig.mode === "hybrid") {



  171
  —
  
    // Hybrid search — combines BM25 keyword matching with vector similarity.



  172
  —
  
    results = await searchHybrid(db, query, queryEmbedding, {



  173
  —
  
      similarity: searchConfig.thresholds.hybridSimilarity,



  174
  —
  
      minVectorSimilarity: searchConfig.thresholds.minVectorSimilarity,



  175
  —
  
      limit: searchConfig.topN,



  176
  —
  
      hybridWeights: searchConfig.hybridWeights,



  177
  —
  
    });



  178
  —
  
  } else {



  179
  —
  
    // Pure vector search



  180
  —
  
    results = await searchVector(db, queryEmbedding, {



  181
  —
  
      similarity: searchConfig.thresholds.vectorSimilarity,



  182
  —
  
      limit: searchConfig.topN,



  183
  —
  
    });



  184
  —
  
  }



  185
  
  



  186
  
  
  // Debug logging for retrieval diagnostics



  187
  —
  
  console.groupCollapsed(



  188
  —
  
    `🔍 Retrieval [${searchConfig.mode}]: "${query.slice(0, 60)}${query.length > 60 ? "..." : ""}"`,



  189
  —
  
  );



  190
  —
  
  console.log(`Hits: ${results.hits?.length ?? 0}`);



  191
  —
  
  console.log(



  192
  —
  
    `Config: threshold=${searchConfig.thresholds[searchConfig.mode === "hybrid" ? "hybridSimilarity" : "vectorSimilarity"]}, topN=${searchConfig.topN}`,



  193
  —
  
  );



  194
  —
  
  if (results.hits) {



  195
  —
  
    results.hits.forEach((hit, i) => {



  196
  —
  
      console.log(



  197
  —
  
        `  [${i + 1}] score=${hit.score.toFixed(3)} | ${hit.document.content.slice(0, 80)}...`,



  198
  —
  
      );



  199
  —
  
    });



  200
  —
  
  }



  201
  —
  
  console.groupEnd();



  202
  
  



  203
  
  
  // Step 3: Build context string from retrieved chunks (or empty if no relevant results)



  204
  —
  
  const contextChunks = results.hits



  205
  —
  
    ? results.hits



  206
  —
  
        .map(



  207
  —
  
          (hit, i) =>



  208
  —
  
            `[Source ${i + 1}: ${hit.document.metadata.sourceFile} (chunk ${hit.document.metadata.chunkIndex})]\n${hit.document.content}`,



  209
  —
  
        )



  210
  —
  
        .join("\n\n---\n\n")



  211
  —
  
    : "";



  212
  
  



  213
  
  
  // Step 4: Build system prompt with context and question



  214
  
  
  // Select prompt based on thinking mode



  215
  —
  
  const promptTemplate = enableThinking



  216
  —
  
    ? SYSTEM_PROMPT_THINKING



  217
  —
  
    : SYSTEM_PROMPT;



  218
  —
  
  let systemPrompt = promptTemplate



  219
  —
  
    .replace("{context}", contextChunks || "(no relevant context found)")



  220
  —
  
    .replace("{question}", query);



  221
  
  



  222
  
  
  // WASM fallback (Qwen3-0.6B): append /think or /no_think based on



  223
  
  
  // the user's thinking toggle.  WebGPU (Qwen3.5) uses chat-template



  224
  
  
  // options instead of prompt flags.



  225
  —
  
  if (getState().hardware.device === "wasm") {



  226
  —
  
    systemPrompt += enableThinking ? "/think" : "/no_think";



  227
  —
  
  }



  228
  
  



  229
  
  
  // Step 5: Build conversation messages with history



  230
  —
  
  const recentHistory = getRecentHistory(MAX_HISTORY);



  231
  —
  
  const messages = [



  232
  —
  
    { role: "system", content: [{ type: "text", text: systemPrompt }] },



  233
  —
  
    ...recentHistory,



  234
  —
  
    { role: "user", content: [{ type: "text", text: query }] },



  235
  —
  
  ];



  236
  
  



  237
  
  
  // Estimate input tokens for tracking



  238
  —
  
  const inputTokens = estimateInputTokens(messages);



  239
  
  



  240
  
  
  // Step 6: Generate response via LLM



  241
  —
  
  const result = await generateResponse(



  242
  —
  
    messages,



  243
  —
  
    onToken,



  244
  —
  
    (fullText, meta) => {



  245
  —
  
      // Update token tracking with actual counts



  246
  —
  
      if (meta?.outputTokenCount !== undefined) {



  247
  —
  
        updateTokenTracking(inputTokens, meta.outputTokenCount);



  248
  —
  
      }



  249
  —
  



  250
  —
  
      if (onComplete) {



  251
  —
  
        onComplete(fullText, {



  252
  —
  
          sourceChunks: results.hits?.map((hit) => hit.document) ?? [],



  253
  —
  
          similarity: results.hits?.map((hit) => hit.score) ?? [],



  254
  —
  
          outputTokenCount: meta?.outputTokenCount ?? 0,



  255
  —
  
        });



  256
  —
  
      }



  257
  —
  
    },



  258
  —
  
    onPhase,



  259
  —
  
  );



  260
  
  



  261
  —
  
  return {



  262
  —
  
    sourceChunks: results.hits?.map((hit) => hit.document) ?? [],



  263
  —
  
    similarity: results.hits?.map((hit) => hit.score) ?? [],



  264
  —
  
  };



  265
  
  
}



  266

// Orama database instance (shared across ingestion and retrieval)



  60
  49
  
let db = null;



  61
  
  



  62

  63
  49
  
const ingestedDocuments = [];



  64
  
  



  65
  
  
// Track whether generation is in progress



  66
  49
  
let isGenerating = false;



  67
  
  



  68
  
  
/**



  69
  
  
 * Get the currently selected chunk size from the radio buttons.



  70
  
  
 * @returns {number} Max tokens per chunk (256, 512, or 1024)



  71
  
  
 */



  72
  
  
function getSelectedChunkSize() {



  73
  —
  
  const selected = document.querySelector('input[name="chunk-size"]:checked');



  74
  —
  
  return selected ? parseInt(selected.value, 10) : 512;



  75
  
  
}



  76
  
  



  77
  
  
/**



  78
  
  
 * Bootstrap the application.



  79
  
  
 * 1. Detect hardware capabilities



  80
  
  
 * 2. Restore persisted index (if any)



  81
  
  
 * 3. Initialize Orama database



  82
  
  
 * 4. Wire up UI callbacks



  83
  
  
 */



  84
  
  
export async function init() {



  85
  
  
  // Initialize i18n before anything else



  86
  49
  
  initI18n();



  87
  
  



  88
  
  
  // Hardware detection



  89
  49
  
  const hardware = await detectHardware();



  90
  49
  
  setState({ hardware });



  91
  
  



  92
  
  
  // Restore persisted index from IndexedDB (always returns a database)



  93
  49
  
  db = await restoreIndex();



  94
  26
  
  console.log(`Index loaded: ${getDocumentCount(db)} documents`);



  95
  
  



  96
  
  
  // If the restored index is empty, try to load the bundled default database



  97
  26
  
  let isDefaultDb = false;



  98
  26
  
  if (getDocumentCount(db) === 0) {



  99
  5
  
    const defaultDb = await loadDefaultDatabase();



  100
  5
  
    if (defaultDb) {

      db = defaultDb;

  4
  
      await persistIndex(db);

  4
  
      isDefaultDb = true;



  104
  4
  
      console.log(`Default database loaded: ${getDocumentCount(db)} documents`);



  105
  5
  
    }



  106
  26
  
  }



  107
  
  



  108
  
  
  // Update index stats in state



  109
  26
  
  setState({



  110
  26
  
    index: {



  111
  26
  
      totalChunks: getDocumentCount(db),



  112
  26
  
      totalDocuments: ingestedDocuments.length,



  113
  26
  
      embeddingDimension: 1024,



  114
  26
  
    },

  26
  
  });
