  // Update document list for restored/default data



  118
  26
  
  if (getDocumentCount(db) > 0) {



  119
  25
  
    updateDocumentList([



  120
  25
  
      {



  121
  25
  
        name: isDefaultDb



  122
  25
  
          ? "Default Database (pre-loaded)"



  123
  25
  
          : "Restored from IndexedDB",



  124
  25
  
        chunks: getDocumentCount(db),



  125
  25
  
        size: 0,



  126
  25
  
      },



  127
  25
  
    ]);



  128
  26
  
  }



  129
  
  



  130
  
  
  // Initialize UI



  131
  26
  
  const { sendBtn, stopBtn, queryInput } = initUI({



  132
  26
  
    onFileUpload: handleFileUpload,



  133
  26
  
    onQuery: handleQuery,



  134
  26
  
    onStop: handleStop,



  135
  26
  
    onLoadModels: handleLoadModels,



  136
  26
  
    onUnloadEmbedding: handleUnloadEmbedding,



  137
  26
  
    onUnloadLLM: handleUnloadLLM,



  138
  26
  
    onClearChat: handleClearChat,



  139
  26
  
    onExportDB: handleExportDB,



  140
  26
  
    onImportDB: handleImportDB,



  141
  26
  
    onClearDB: handleClearDB,



  142
  26
  
    onResetSearchConfig: handleResetSettings,



  143
  26
  
    onResetLlmConfig: handleResetLlmSettings,



  144
  26
  
  });



  145
  
  



  146
  
  
  // Wire warning banner clear-chat button



  147
  26
  
  document



  148
  26
  
    .getElementById("clear-chat-warn-btn")



  149
  26
  
    .addEventListener("click", () => {



  150
  —
  
      handleClearChat();



  151
  26
  
    });



  152
  
  



  153
  
  
  // Subscribe to state changes for UI updates



  154
  26
  
  subscribe(updateStatusBar);



  155
  26
  
  subscribe(updateModelState);



  156
  
  



  157
  
  
  // Update status bar immediately



  158
  26
  
  updateStatusBar(getState());



  159
  26
  
  updateModelState(getState());



  160
  
  



  161
  
  
  // Register service worker if available



  162
  26
  
  if ("serviceWorker" in navigator) {



  163
  26
  
    navigator.serviceWorker.register("/sw.js").catch(() => {



  164
  26
  
      // Silently fail — SW is optional for offline support



  165
  26
  
    });



  166
  26
  
  }



  167
  
  



  168
  26
  
  console.log("RAG-Browser initialized.");



  169
  
  
}



  170
  
  



  171
  
  
/**



  172
  
  
 * Handle file upload: validate, ingest, and update UI.



  173
  
  
 * @param {File[]} files



  174
  
  
 */



  175
  
  
async function handleFileUpload(files) {



  176
  —
  
  for (const file of files) {



  177
  —
  
    if (!isSupportedFormat(file.name)) {



  178
  —
  
      const ext = file.name.includes(".")



  179
  —
  
        ? file.name.slice(file.name.lastIndexOf(".")).toLowerCase()



  180
  —
  
        : "";



  181
  —
  
      let message;



  182
  —
  
      if (ext === ".doc") {



  183
  —
  
        message = `Skipping ${file.name}: .doc (binary) is unsupported in-browser. Please convert to .docx or .txt first.`;



  184
  —
  
      } else if (ext === ".ppt") {



  185
  —
  
        message = `Skipping ${file.name}: .ppt (binary) is unsupported in-browser. Please convert to .pptx or .txt first.`;



  186
  —
  
      } else {



  187
  —
  
        message = `Skipping ${file.name}: unsupported format. Supported: ${SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS.join(", ")}`;



  188
  —
  
      }



  189
  —
  
      showNotification(message, "warning");



  190
  —
  
      continue;



  191
  —
  
    }



  192
  —
  



  193
  —
  
    // Load embedding model if not loaded



  194
  —
  
    if (!isEmbeddingLoaded()) {



  195
  —
  
      showNotification(t("notif.loadingEmbedding"), "info");



  196
  —
  
      setState({ models: { ...getState().models, embedding: "loading" } });



  197
  —
  



  198
  —
  
      try {



  199
  —
  
        await loadEmbeddingModel(getState().hardware);



  200
  —
  
        setState({ models: { ...getState().models, embedding: "ready" } });



  201
  —
  
      } catch (error) {



  202
  —
  
        console.error("Failed to load embedding model:", error);



  203
  —
  
        setState({ models: { ...getState().models, embedding: "unloaded" } });



  204
  —
  
        showNotification(



  205
  —
  
          t("notif.failedEmbedding", { error: error.message || error }),



  206
  —
  
          "error",



  207
  —
  
        );



  208
  —
  
        return;



  209
  —
  
      }



  210
  —
  
    }



  211
  —
  



  212
  —
  
    // Ingest document



  213
  —
  
    try {



  214
  —
  
      const result = await ingestDocument(



  215
  —
  
        file,



  216
  —
  
        db,



  217
  —
  
        (progress) => {



  218
  —
  
          updateProgress(progress);



  219
  —
  
        },



  220
  —
  
        { maxTokens: getSelectedChunkSize() },



  221
  —
  
      );



  222
  —
  



  223
  —
  
      // Update state and UI



  224
  —
  
      ingestedDocuments.push({



  225
  —
  
        name: file.name,



  226
  —
  
        chunks: result.chunks,



  227
  —
  
        size: result.fileSize,



  228
  —
  
      });



  229
  —
  



  230
  —
  
      setState({



  231
  —
  
        index: {



  232
  —
  
          totalChunks: getDocumentCount(db),



  233
  —
  
          totalDocuments: ingestedDocuments.length,



  234
  —
  
          embeddingDimension: 1024,



  235
  —
  
        },



  236
  —
  
      });



  237
  —
  



  238
  —
  
      updateDocumentList(ingestedDocuments);



  239
  —
  



  240
  —
  
      // Persist index to IndexedDB



  241
  —
  
      await persistIndex(db);



  242
  —
  



  243
  —
  
      showNotification(t("notif.fileIndexed", { file: file.name }), "info");



  244
  —
  
    } catch (error) {



  245
  —
  
      console.error("Ingestion failed:", error);



  246
  —
  
      showNotification(



  247
  —
  
        t("notif.fileIndexFailed", { file: file.name, error: error.message }),



  248
  —
  
        "error",



  249
  —
  
      );



  250
  —
  
    }



  251
  —
  
  }



  252
  
  
}



  253
  
  



  254
  
  
/**



  255
  
  
 * Handle user query: render message, retrieve context, generate response.



  256
  
  
 * @param {string} query



  257
  
  
 */



  258
  
  
async function handleQuery(query) {



  259
  —
  
  if (isGenerating) return;



  260
  
  



  261
  
  
  // Render user message



  262
  —
  
  renderUserMessage(query);



  263
  
  



  264
  
  
  // Add to conversation history



  265
  —
  
  addMessage("user", query);



  266
  
  



  267
  
  
  // Disable send button, show stop button



  268
  —
  
  const { sendBtn, stopBtn, queryInput } = getUIButtons();



  269
  —
  
  sendBtn.disabled = true;



  270
  —
  
  stopBtn.style.display = "inline-block";



  271
  —
  
  queryInput.disabled = true;



  272
  —
  
  isGenerating = true;



  273
  
  



  274
  —
  
  try {



  275
  —
  
    // Load LLM if not loaded



  276
  —
  
    if (!isLLMLoaded()) {



  277
  —
  
      setState({ models: { ...getState().models, llm: "loading" } });



  278
  —
  
      try {



  279
  —
  
        await loadLLM(getState().hardware);



  280
  —
  
        setState({ models: { ...getState().models, llm: "ready" } });



  281
  —
  
      } catch (error) {



  282
  —
  
        console.error("Failed to load LLM:", error);



  283
  —
  
        setState({ models: { ...getState().models, llm: "unloaded" } });



  284
  —
  
        showNotification(



  285
  —
  
          `Failed to load LLM: ${error.message || error}`,



  286
  —
  
          "error",



  287
  —
  
        );



  288
  —
  
        resetUIButtons();



  289
  —
  
        return;



  290
  —
  
      }



  291
  —
  
    }



  292
  —
  



  293
  —
  
    // Check if we have documents to search



  294
  —
  
    if (getDocumentCount(db) === 0) {



  295
  —
  
      const emptyMsg = renderStreamingMessage();



  296
  —
  
      emptyMsg.onToken(t("notif.noDocuments"));



  297
  —
  
      emptyMsg.finalize();



  298
  —
  
      addMessage("assistant", t("notif.noDocuments"));



  299
  —
  
      return;



  300
  —
  
    }



  301
  —
  



  302
  —
  
    // Create streaming message renderer



  303
  —
  
    const messageRenderer = renderStreamingMessage();



  304
  —
  



  305
  —
  
    // Show loading state while model generates (no more streaming tokens)



  306
  —
  
    messageRenderer.showThinking();



  307
  —
  



  308
  —
  
    // Run retrieval + generation pipeline



  309
  —
  
    let completed = false;



  310
  —
  
    const pipelineResult = await retrieveAndGenerate(



  311
  —
  
      query,



  312
  —
  
      db,



  313
  —
  
      messageRenderer.onToken,



  314
  —
  
      (fullText, { sourceChunks, similarity }) => {



  315
  —
  
        // onComplete callback — sourceChunks and similarity are passed



  316
  —
  
        // as arguments here, avoiding the temporal dead zone problem



  317
  —
  
        // that occurs when referencing the destructured variables before



  318
  —
  
        // the await resolves.



  319
  —
  
        completed = true;



  320
  —
  
        messageRenderer.finalize(sourceChunks, similarity);



  321
  —
  
        addMessage("assistant", fullText, sourceChunks);



  322
  —
  
      },



  323
  —
  
      messageRenderer.onPhase,



  324
  —
  
    );



  325
  —
  



  326
  —
  
    const { sourceChunks, similarity } = pipelineResult;



  327
  —
  



  328
  —
  
    // If onComplete wasn't called (e.g. generation was stopped), finalize with partial text



  329
  —
  
    if (!completed) {



  330
  —
  
      const partialText = messageRenderer.getFullText();



  331
  —
  
      if (partialText) {



  332
  —
  
        messageRenderer.finalize(sourceChunks, similarity);



  333
  —
  
        addMessage("assistant", partialText, sourceChunks);



  334
  —
  
      }



  335
  —
  
    }



  336
  —
  
  } catch (error) {



  337
  —
  
    console.error("Query failed:", error);



  338
  —
  
    showNotification(t("notif.queryFailed", { error: error.message }), "error");



  339
  —
  
  } finally {



  340
  —
  
    resetUIButtons();



  341
  —
  
  }



  342
  
  
}



  343
  
  



  344
  
  
/**



  345
  
  
 * Handle stop generation.



  346
  
  
 */



  347
  
  
function handleStop() {



  348
  —
  
  stopGeneration();



  349
  
  
}



  350
  
  



  351
  
  
/**



  352
  
  
 * Handle explicit model loading.



  353
  
  
 */



  354
  
  
async function handleLoadModels() {



  355
  —
  
  const config = getState().hardware;



  356
  
  



  357
  
  
  // Show loading modal



  358
  —
  
  showLoadingModal();



  359
  
  



  360
  
  
  // Load embedding model



  361
  —
  
  if (!isEmbeddingLoaded()) {



  362
  —
  
    setState({ models: { ...getState().models, embedding: "loading" } });



  363
  —
  



  364
  —
  
    try {



  365
  —
  
      await loadEmbeddingModel(config, (info) => {



  366
  —
  
        onModelProgress("embedding", info);



  367
  —
  
      });



  368
  —
  
      setState({ models: { ...getState().models, embedding: "ready" } });



  369
  —
  
      updateLoadingModal("embedding", 100, "ready");



  370
  —
  
    } catch (error) {



  371
  —
  
      console.error("Failed to load embedding model:", error);



  372
  —
  
      setState({ models: { ...getState().models, embedding: "unloaded" } });



  373
  —
  
      showNotification(



  374
  —
  
        t("notif.failedEmbedding", { error: error.message || error }),



  375
  —
  
        "error",



  376
  —
  
      );



  377
  —
  
      hideLoadingModal();



  378
  —
  
      return;



  379
  —
  
    }



  380
  —
  
  } else {



  381
  —
  
    // Already loaded - mark as done



  382
  —
  
    updateLoadingModal("embedding", 100, "ready");



  383
  —
  
  }



  384
  
  



  385
  
  
  // Load LLM



  386
  —
  
  if (!isLLMLoaded()) {



  387
  —
  
    setState({ models: { ...getState().models, llm: "loading" } });



  388
  —
  



  389
  —
  
    try {



  390
  —
  
      await loadLLM(config, (info) => {



  391
  —
  
        onModelProgress("llm", info);



  392
  —
  
      });



  393
  —
  



  394
  —
  
      // Apply model-specific settings after successful load



  395
  —
  
      const contextWindow = getContextWindow();



  396
  —
  
      setContextWindow(contextWindow);



  397
  —
  
      resetTokenTracking();



  398
  —
  



  399
  —
  
      // Auto-disable thinking mode if the loaded model doesn't support it



  400
  —
  
      if (!supportsThinking()) {



  401
  —
  
        setLlmConfig({ enableThinking: false });



  402
  —
  
      }



  403
  —
  



  404
  —
  
      setState({ models: { ...getState().models, llm: "ready" } });



  405
  —
  
      updateLoadingModal("llm", 100, "ready");



  406
  —
  
    } catch (error) {



  407
  —
  
      console.error("Failed to load LLM:", error);



  408
  —
  
      setState({ models: { ...getState().models, llm: "unloaded" } });



  409
  —
  
      showNotification(



  410
  —
  
        t("notif.failedLlm", { error: error.message || error }),



  411
  —
  
        "error",



  412
  —
  
      );



  413
  —
  
      hideLoadingModal();



  414
  —
  
      return;



  415
  —
  
    }



  416
  —
  
  } else {



  417
  —
  
    // Already loaded - mark as done



  418
  —
  
    updateLoadingModal("llm", 100, "ready");



  419
  —
  
  }



  420
  
  



  421
  
  
  // Small delay so user sees the final state



  422
  —
  
  await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 600));



  423
  —
  
  hideLoadingModal();



  424
  —
  
  showNotification(t("notif.modelsLoaded"), "info");



  425
  
  
}



  426
  
  



  427
  
  
/**



  428
  
  
 * Process progress callback info from transformers.js pipeline() and update the modal.



  429
  
  
 * @param {'embedding'|'llm'} step - Which model is loading



  430
  
  
 * @param {Object} info - Progress info from transformers.js



  431
  
  
 */



  432
  
  
function onModelProgress(step, info) {



  433
  —
  
  if (info.status === "progress_total") {



  434
  —
  
    updateLoadingModal(step, info.progress);



  435
  —
  
  } else if (info.status === "download") {



  436
  —
  
    const progress = info.total ? (info.loaded / info.total) * 100 : 0;



  437
  —
  
    updateLoadingModal(step, progress, info.status, info.name);



  438
  —
  
  } else if (info.status === "init") {



  439
  —
  
    updateLoadingModal(step, 0, info.status);



  440
  —
  
  }



  441
  
  
}



  442
  
  



  443
  
  
/**



  444
  
  
 * React to model state changes: enable UI elements based on which models are loaded.



  445
  
  
 * - File uploads require only the embedding model (for generating embeddings).



  446
  
  
 * - Querying requires both embedding and LLM models.



  447
  
  
 * @param {Object} state - Current application state



  448
  
  
 */



  449
  
  
function updateModelState(state) {



  450
  29
  
  const { embedding, llm } = state.models;



  451
  29
  
  const embeddingReady = embedding === "ready";



  452
  29
  
  const llmReady = llm === "ready";



  453
  
  



  454
  
  
  // File input: only embedding model required



  455
  29
  
  setFileInputEnabled(embeddingReady);



  456
  
  



  457
  
  
  // Send button + query input: both models required, but keep disabled during generation



  458
  29
  
  setSendButtonEnabled(embeddingReady && llmReady, isGenerating);



  459
  
  



  460
  
  
  // Unload buttons: enabled when their respective model is loaded



  461
  29
  
  setUnloadButtonStates(embeddingReady, llmReady);



  462
  
  
}



  463
  
  



  464
  
  
/**



  465
  
  
 * Handle embedding model unload.



  466
  
  
 *



  467
  
  
 * Because ONNX Runtime Web shares a single global WebGPU device across all



  468
  
  
 * inference sessions, unloading one model does NOT free its VRAM while the



  469
  
  
 * other model's session still holds the device. To actually free VRAM we



  470
  
  
 * unload BOTH models (which destroys the device) and then reload the LLM



  471
  
  
 * so the user's remaining model is available immediately.



  472
  
  
 */



  473
  
  
async function handleUnloadEmbedding() {



  474
  —
  
  const otherWasLoaded = isLLMLoaded();



  475
  
  



  476
  —
  
  await unloadEmbeddingModel();



  477
  —
  
  await unloadLLM();



  478
  —
  
  setState({



  479
  —
  
    models: {



  480
  —
  
      ...getState().models,



  481
  —
  
      embedding: "unloaded",



  482
  —
  
      llm: otherWasLoaded ? "loading" : "unloaded",



  483
  —
  
    },



  484
  —
  
  });



  485
  
  



  486
  
  
  // Show notification immediately



  487
  —
  
  showNotification(t("notif.embeddingUnloaded"), "info");



  488
  
  



  489
  
  
  // If the LLM was loaded, reload it so the user can keep using it



  490
  —
  
  if (otherWasLoaded) {



  491
  —
  
    try {



  492
  —
  
      await loadLLM(getState().hardware);



  493
  —
  
      setState({ models: { ...getState().models, llm: "ready" } });



  494
  —
  
    } catch (error) {



  495
  —
  
      console.error("Failed to reload LLM:", error);



  496
  —
  
      setState({ models: { ...getState().models, llm: "unloaded" } });



  497
  —
  
      showNotification(t("notif.reloadLlmFailed"), "error");



  498
  —
  
    }



  499
  —
  
  }



  500
  
  
}



  501
  
  



  502
  
  
/**



  503
  
  
 * Handle LLM unload.



  504
  
  
 *



  505
  
  
 * Same shared-WebGPU-device reasoning as handleUnloadEmbedding — unload both



  506
  
  
 * to destroy the device and actually free VRAM, then reload the embedding



  507
  
  
 * model so the user can keep indexing documents.



  508
  
  
 */



  509
  
  
async function handleUnloadLLM() {



  510
  —
  
  const otherWasLoaded = isEmbeddingLoaded();



  511
  
  



  512
  —
  
  await unloadLLM();



  513
  —
  
  await unloadEmbeddingModel();



  514
  —
  
  setState({



  515
  —
  
    models: {



  516
  —
  
      ...getState().models,



  517
  —
  
      llm: "unloaded",



  518
  —
  
      embedding: otherWasLoaded ? "loading" : "unloaded",



  519
  —
  
    },



  520
  —
  
  });



  521
  
  



  522
  
  
  // Show notification immediately



  523
  —
  
  showNotification(t("notif.llmUnloaded"), "info");



  524
  
  



  525
  
  
  // If the embedding model was loaded, reload it so the user can keep using it



  526
  —
  
  if (otherWasLoaded) {



  527
  —
  
    try {



  528
  —
  
      await loadEmbeddingModel(getState().hardware);



  529
  —
  
      setState({ models: { ...getState().models, embedding: "ready" } });



  530
  —
  
    } catch (error) {



  531
  —
  
      console.error("Failed to reload embedding model:", error);



  532
  —
  
      setState({ models: { ...getState().models, embedding: "unloaded" } });



  533
  —
  
      showNotification(t("notif.reloadEmbeddingFailed"), "error");



  534
  —
  
    }



  535
  —
  
  }



  536
  
  
}



  537
  
  



  538
  
  
/**



  539
  
  
 * Handle clear chat.



  540
  
  
 */



  541
  
  
function handleClearChat() {



  542
  —
  
  clearConversation();



  543
  —
  
  resetTokenTracking();



  544
  —
  
  document.getElementById("conversation").innerHTML = "";



  545
  —
  
  showNotification(t("notif.chatCleared"), "info");



  546
  
  
}



  547
  
  



  548
  
  
/**



  549
  
  
 * Handle database clear: confirmation, then wipe in-memory DB,



  550
  
  
 * IndexedDB persistence, ingested document list, and conversation.



  551
  
  
 */



  552
  
  
async function handleClearDB() {



  553
  —
  
  const count = getDocumentCount(db);



  554
  —
  
  const confirmed = confirm(



  555
  —
  
    t("notif.dbClearConfirm", { count }) + "\n\n" + t("notif.cannotUndo"),



  556
  —
  
  );



  557
  —
  
  if (!confirmed) {



  558
  —
  
    return;



  559
  —
  
  }



  560
  
  



  561
  
  
  // Replace in-memory database with a fresh empty instance



  562
  —
  
  db = createDB();



  563
  
  



  564
  
  
  // Clear ingested document list



  565
  —
  
  ingestedDocuments.length = 0;



  566
  
  



  567
  
  
  // Clear conversation



  568
  —
  
  clearConversation();



  569
  —
  
  const conversationEl = document.getElementById("conversation");



  570
  —
  
  if (conversationEl) {



  571
  —
  
    conversationEl.innerHTML = "";



  572
  —
  
  }



  573
  
  



  574
  
  
  // Update state



  575
  —
  
  setState({



  576
  —
  
    index: {



  577
  —
  
      totalChunks: 0,



  578
  —
  
      totalDocuments: 0,



  579
  —
  
      embeddingDimension: 1024,



  580
  —
  
    },



  581
  —
  
  });



  582
  
  



  583
  
  
  // Clear document list UI



  584
  —
  
  updateDocumentList([]);



  585
  
  



  586
  
  
  // Persist empty state to IndexedDB (clears previous data)



  587
  —
  
  try {



  588
  —
  
    await persistIndex(db);



  589
  —
  
  } catch (error) {



  590
  —
  
    console.error("Failed to persist empty index:", error);



  591
  —
  
  }



  592
  
  



  593
  —
  
  showNotification(t("notif.dbClearedWithCount", { count }), "info");



  594
  
  
}



  595
  
  



  596
  
  
/**



  597
  
  
 * Handle database export: serialize the Orama DB to a versioned JSON file



  598
  
  
 * and trigger a browser download.



  599
  
  
 */



  600
  
  
function handleExportDB() {



  601
  —
  
  if (!db || getDocumentCount(db) === 0) {



  602
  —
  
    showNotification(t("notif.noDataExport"), "warning");



  603
  —
  
    return;



  604
  —
  
  }



  605
  
  



  606
  —
  
  try {



  607
  —
  
    const count = getDocumentCount(db);



  608
  —
  
    const blob = serializeDB(db);



  609
  —
  
    const filename = generateExportFilename(count);



  610
  —
  



  611
  —
  
    // Trigger download via temporary anchor element



  612
  —
  
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);



  613
  —
  
    const a = document.createElement("a");



  614
  —
  
    a.href = url;



  615
  —
  
    a.download = filename;



  616
  —
  
    document.body.appendChild(a);



  617
  —
  
    a.click();



  618
  —
  
    document.body.removeChild(a);



  619
  —
  
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);



  620
  —
  



  621
  —
  
    showNotification(t("notif.exported", { count, filename }), "info");



  622
  —
  
  } catch (error) {



  623
  —
  
    console.error("Export failed:", error);



  624
  —
  
    showNotification(



  625
  —
  
      t("notif.exportFailed", { error: error.message }),



  626
  —
  
      "error",



  627
  —
  
    );



  628
  —
  
  }



  629
  
  
}



  630
  
  



  631
  
  
/**



  632
  
  
 * Handle database import: read a JSON file, validate it, and replace



  633
  
  
 * the current Orama database if the user confirms.



  634
  
  
 */



  635
  
  
async function handleImportDB(file) {



  636
  —
  
  console.log("Importing database from:", file.name);



  637
  
  



  638
  —
  
  if (!file.name.endsWith(".json")) {



  639
  —
  
    showNotification(t("notif.invalidFileType"), "error");



  640
  —
  
    return;



  641
  —
  
  }



  642
  
  



  643
  —
  
  try {



  644
  —
  
    const text = await file.text();



  645
  —
  
    const parsed = JSON.parse(text);



  646
  —
  



  647
  —
  
    // Validate the imported data



  648
  —
  
    const result = validateImport(parsed);



  649
  —
  
    if (!result.valid) {



  650
  —
  
      showNotification(



  651
  —
  
        t("notif.importValidationFailed", { error: result.error }),



  652
  —
  
        "error",



  653
  —
  
      );



  654
  —
  
      return;



  655
  —
  
    }



  656
  —
  



  657
  —
  
    const { rawData, metadata } = result;



  658
  —
  
    // Count documents from metadata or raw structure. Cannot use getDocumentCount()



  659
  —
  
    // here because rawData is serialized state, not a live Orama instance.



  660
  —
  
    let importCount = metadata.documentCount || 0;



  661
  —
  
    if (!importCount && rawData.docs) {



  662
  —
  
      const docs = rawData.docs;



  663
  —
  
      importCount = Array.isArray(docs)



  664
  —
  
        ? docs.length



  665
  —
  
        : Object.values(docs).length;



  666
  —
  
    }



  667
  —
  



  668
  —
  
    // Check current DB size and warn about data loss



  669
  —
  
    const currentCount = db ? getDocumentCount(db) : 0;



  670
  —
  
    if (currentCount > 0) {



  671
  —
  
      const confirmed = confirm(



  672
  —
  
        t("notif.importReplaceConfirm", {



  673
  —
  
          current: currentCount,



  674
  —
  
          imported: importCount,



  675
  —
  
        }),



  676
  —
  
      );



  677
  —
  
      if (!confirmed) {



  678
  —
  
        showNotification(t("notif.importCancelled"), "info");



  679
  —
  
        return;



  680
  —
  
      }



  681
  —
  
    }



  682
  —
  



  683
  —
  
    // Restore into a fresh, fully functional Orama database using load()



  684
  —
  
    db = await restoreFromData(rawData);



  685
  —
  
    ingestedDocuments.length = 0; // Clear old document list



  686
  —
  



  687
  —
  
    // Update state and UI



  688
  —
  
    setState({



  689
  —
  
      index: {



  690
  —
  
        totalChunks: getDocumentCount(db),



  691
  —
  
        totalDocuments: 0,



  692
  —
  
        embeddingDimension: 1024,



  693
  —
  
      },



  694
  —
  
    });



  695
  —
  



  696
  —
  
    updateDocumentList([



  697
  —
  
      {



  698
  —
  
        name: `Imported (${metadata.exportedAt ? new Date(metadata.exportedAt).toLocaleString() : file.name})`,



  699
  —
  
        chunks: importCount,



  700
  —
  
        size: 0,



  701
  —
  
      },



  702
  —
  
    ]);



  703
  —
  



  704
  —
  
    // Persist to IndexedDB



  705
  —
  
    await persistIndex(db);



  706
  —
  



  707
  —
  
    showNotification(



  708
  —
  
      t("notif.imported", { count: importCount, file: file.name }),



  709
  —
  
      "info",



  710
  —
  
    );



  711
  —
  
  } catch (error) {



  712
  —
  
    console.error("Import failed:", error);



  713
  —
  
    if (error instanceof SyntaxError) {



  714
  —
  
      showNotification(t("notif.invalidJson"), "error");



  715
  —
  
    } else {



  716
  —
  
      showNotification(



  717
  —
  
        t("notif.importFailed", { error: error.message }),



  718
  —
  
        "error",



  719
  —
  
      );



  720
  —
  
    }



  721
  —
  
  }



  722
  
  
}



  723
  
  



  724
  
  
/**



  725
  
  
 * Get references to UI buttons (used in async handlers).



  726
  
  
 */



  727
  
  
function getUIButtons() {



  728
  —
  
  return {



  729
  —
  
    sendBtn: document.getElementById("send-btn"),



  730
  —
  
    stopBtn: document.getElementById("stop-btn"),



  731
  —
  
    queryInput: document.getElementById("query-input"),



  732
  —
  
  };



  733
  
  
}



  734
  
  



  735
  
  
/**



  736
  
  
 * Reset UI buttons after query generation completes.



  737
  
  
 * Re-enables send button and query input based on current model state.



  738
  
  
 */



  739
  
  
function resetUIButtons() {



  740
  —
  
  const { stopBtn } = getUIButtons();



  741
  —
  
  stopBtn.style.display = "none";



  742
  
  



  743
  
  
  // Re-evaluate button states based on model availability (generation is now complete)



  744
  —
  
  isGenerating = false;



  745
  —
  
  updateModelState(getState());



  746
  
  
}



  747
  
  



  748
  
  
/**



  749
  
  
 * Handle search settings reset.



  750
  
  
 */



  751
  
  
function handleResetSettings() {



  752
  —
  
  syncSettingsUI();



  753
  —
  
  showNotification(t("notif.settingsReset"), "info");



  754
  
  
}



  755
  
  



  756
  
  
/**



  757
  
  
 * Handle LLM settings reset.



  758
  
  
 */



  759
  
  
function handleResetLlmSettings() {



  760
  —
  
  syncLlmSettingsUI();



  761
  —
  
  showNotification(t("notif.llmSettingsReset"), "info");



  762
  
  
}



  763
  
  



  764
  
  
// Bootstrap



  765
  49
  
init();



  766

// renderer.js - Markdown and LaTeX rendering for chat messages



  2
  
  



  3
  
  
import { marked } from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/marked/lib/marked.esm.js";



  4
  
  
import renderMathInElement from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.17.0/dist/contrib/auto-render.mjs";



  5
  
  



  6
  
  
// Configure marked for chat output



  7
  49
  
marked.setOptions({



  8
  49
  
  gfm: true,



  9
  49
  
  breaks: true,



  10
  49
  
});



  11
  
  



  12
  
  
// Configure link renderer to open in new tab



  13
  49
  
const renderer = new marked.Renderer();



  14
  49
  
renderer.link = function (href, title, text) {



  15
  —
  
  const hrefAttr = href ? href.replace(/^javascript:/gi, "") : "";



  16
  49
  
  let html =



  17
  —
  
    '<a href="' + hrefAttr + '" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"';



  18
  —
  
  if (title) html += ' title="' + title + '"';



  19
  —
  
  html += '">' + text + "</a>";



  20
  —
  
  return html;



  21
  49
  
};



  22
  
  



  23
  
  
/**



  24
  
  
 * Pre-process raw model output to handle think tags before markdown parsing.



  25
  
  
 * Collapses model "thinking" blocks into expandable details elements.



  26
  
  
 * The thinking content itself is rendered as markdown for readability.



  27
  
  
 */



  28
  
  
function preprocessThinking(content) {



  29
  —
  
  if (!content) return "";



  30
  
  



  31
  
  
  // Build regex dynamically to avoid tag issues



  32
  —
  
  var openTag = "<" + "think>";



  33
  —
  
  var closeTag = "<" + "/think>";



  34
  —
  
  var regex = new RegExp(openTag + "([\\s\\S]*?)" + closeTag, "gi");



  35
  
  



  36
  —
  
  return content.replace(regex, function (_match, thought) {



  37
  —
  
    // Render thinking content as markdown so bold, lists, code, etc. appear correctly



  38
  —
  
    var renderedThought = marked.parse(thought.trim());



  39
  —
  
    return (



  40
  —
  
      '<details class="think-block"><summary>Thinking Process</summary><div class="think-content">' +



  41
  —
  
      renderedThought +



  42
  —
  
      "</div></details>"



  43
  —
  
    );



  44
  —
  
  });



  45
  
  
}



  46
  
  



  47
  
  
/**



  48
  
  
 * Render markdown content to HTML string.



  49
  
  
 * @param {string} content - Raw markdown text



  50
  
  
 * @returns {string} Rendered HTML



  51
  
  
 */



  52
  
  
export function renderMarkdown(content) {



  53
  —
  
  if (!content) return "";



  54
  —
  
  const preprocessed = preprocessThinking(content);



  55
  —
  
  return marked.parse(preprocessed);



  56
  
  
}



  57
  
  



  58
  
  
/**



  59
  
  
 * Render LaTeX in an already-rendered DOM element using KaTeX auto-render.



  60
  
  
 * Scans for $$...$$ (display math) and $...$ (inline math).



  61
  
  
 * Skips <pre>, <code>, <script> tags to avoid breaking code blocks.



  62
  
  
 * @param {HTMLElement} element - DOM element containing rendered markdown



  63
  
  
 */



  64
  
  
export function renderLaTeX(element) {



  65
  —
  
  if (!element || typeof renderMathInElement !== "function") return;



  66
  
  



  67
  —
  
  renderMathInElement(element, {



  68
  —
  
    delimiters: [



  69
  —
  
      { left: "$$", right: "$$", display: true },



  70
  —
  
      { left: "$", right: "$", display: false },



  71
  —
  
    ],



  72
  —
  
    throwOnError: false,



  73
  —
  
    ignoreClass: "katex-ignore",



  74
  —
  
  });



  75
  
  
}



  76

  1
  
  
// fileParser.js — Unified file parser for RAG document ingestion



  2
  
  



  3
  
  
/**



  4
  
  
 * All supported file extensions for document ingestion.



  5
  
  
 */



  6
  49
  
export const SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS = [



  7
  49
  
  // Plain text



  8
  49
  
  ".txt",



  9
  49
  
  // Markdown



  10
  49
  
  ".md",



  11
  49
  
  // Spreadsheet



  12
  49
  
  ".csv",



  13
  49
  
  ".xls",



  14
  49
  
  ".xlsx",



  15
  49
  
  // Word (modern only — binary .doc is unsupported)



  16
  49
  
  ".docx",



  17
  49
  
  // PowerPoint (modern only — binary .ppt is unsupported)



  18
  49
  
  ".pptx",



  19
  49
  
  // OpenDocument formats



  20
  49
  
  ".odt",



  21
  49
  
  ".ods",



  22
  49
  
  ".odp",



  23
  49
  
  // PDF



  24
  49
  
  ".pdf",



  25
  49
  
];



  26
  
  



  27
  
  
/**



  28
  
  
 * Extensions that require the officeParser library (non-plain-text formats).



  29
  
  
 * .txt and .md are handled natively without any external dependency.



  30
  
  
 */



  31
  49
  
const OFFICE_EXTENSIONS = [



  32
  49
  
  ".csv",



  33
  49
  
  ".xls",



  34
  49
  
  ".xlsx",



  35
  49
  
  ".docx",



  36
  49
  
  ".pptx",



  37
  49
  
  ".odt",



  38
  49
  
  ".ods",



  39
  49
  
  ".odp",



  40
  49
  
];



  41
  
  



  42
  
  
/**



  43
  
  
 * Extensions that require the PDF.js library for parsing.



  44
  
  
 */



  45
  49
  
const PDF_EXTENSIONS = [".pdf"];



  46
  
  



  47
  
  
/**



  48
  
  
 * CDN URL for the officeParser browser IIFE bundle.



  49
  
  
 * Pinned to version 7.2.0 for stability.



  50
  
  
 */



  51
  
  
const OFFICE_PARSER_CDN =



  52
  49
  
  "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/officeparser@7.2.0/dist/officeparser.browser.iife.js";



  53
  
  



  54
  
  
// ─── State ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────



  55
  
  



  56
  
  
/** @type {Promise<boolean>|null} — Resolves when officeParser is loaded */



  57
  49
  
let officeParserLoadPromise = null;



  58
  
  



  59
  
  
/** @type {Promise<object>|null} — Resolves when PDF.js is loaded */



  60
  49
  
let pdfjsLoadPromise = null;



  61
  
  



  62
  
  
/**



  63
  
  
 * PDF.js version pinned for stability. Uses jsDelivr CDN.



  64
  
  
 */



  65
  49
  
const PDFJS_VERSION = "4.9.155";



  66
  49
  
const PDFJS_CDN_BASE = `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/pdfjs-dist@${PDFJS_VERSION}/build`;



  67
  
  



  68
  
  
/**



  69
  
  
 * Load officeParser from CDN as an IIFE script. Returns true when loaded.



  70
  
  
 * Lazy-loaded on first demand; subsequent calls reuse the same promise.



  71
  
  
 *



  72
  
  
 * @returns {Promise<boolean>}



  73
  
  
 */



  74
  
  
export async function loadOfficeParser() {



  75
  —
  
  if (officeParserLoadPromise !== null) {



  76
  —
  
    return officeParserLoadPromise;



  77
  —
  
  }



  78
  
  



  79
  —
  
  officeParserLoadPromise = (async () => {



  80
  —
  
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {



  81
  —
  
      const script = document.createElement("script");



  82
  —
  
      script.src = OFFICE_PARSER_CDN;



  83
  —
  
      script.onload = () => resolve(true);



  84
  —
  
      script.onerror = () =>



  85
  —
  
        reject(



  86
  —
  
          new Error(



  87
  —
  
            `Failed to load officeParser from CDN (${OFFICE_PARSER_CDN})`,



  88
  —
  
          ),



  89
  —
  
        );



  90
  —
  
      document.head.appendChild(script);



  91
  —
  
    });



  92
  —
  
  })();



  93
  
  



  94
  —
  
  return officeParserLoadPromise;



  95
  
  
}



  96
  
  



  97
  
  
// ─── Format Checks ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────



  98
  
  



  99
  
  
/**



  100
  
  
 * Check if a filename has a supported extension.



  101
  
  
 *



  102
  
  
 * @param {string} filename



  103
  
  
 * @returns {boolean}



  104
  
  
 */



  105
  
  
export function isSupportedFormat(filename) {



  106
  —
  
  const ext = getExtension(filename);



  107
  —
  
  return SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS.includes(ext);



  108
  
  
}



  109
  
  



  110
  
  
/**



  111
  
  
 * Check if a file requires the officeParser library (non-plain-text).



  112
  
  
 *



  113
  
  
 * @param {string} filename



  114
  
  
 * @returns {boolean}



  115
  
  
 */



  116
  
  
export function needsOfficeParser(filename) {



  117
  —
  
  const ext = getExtension(filename);



  118
  —
  
  return OFFICE_EXTENSIONS.includes(ext);



  119
  
  
}



  120
  
  



  121
  
  
/**



  122
  
  
 * Check if a file requires the PDF.js library.



  123
  
  
 *



  124
  
  
 * @param {string} filename



  125
  
  
 * @returns {boolean}



  126
  
  
 */



  127
  
  
export function needsPdfJs(filename) {



  128
  —
  
  const ext = getExtension(filename);



  129
  —
  
  return PDF_EXTENSIONS.includes(ext);



  130
  
  
}



  131
  
  



  132
  
  
/**



  133
  
  
 * Get the file extension including the dot (e.g. ".xlsx").



  134
  
  
 *



  135
  
  
 * @param {string} filename



  136
  
  
 * @returns {string}



  137
  
  
 */



  138
  
  
function getExtension(filename) {



  139
  —
  
  const lastDot = filename.lastIndexOf(".");



  140
  —
  
  return lastDot === -1 ? "" : filename.slice(lastDot).toLowerCase();



  141
  
  
}



  142
  
  



  143
  
  
// ─── Parsing ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────



  144
  
  



  145
  
  
/**



  146
  
  
 * Parse a file and extract its plain text content.



  147
  
  
 *



  148
  
  
 * For .txt and .md files: reads directly with File.text(), optionally



  149
  
  
 * stripping Markdown syntax for .md files.



  150
  
  
 * For .pdf files: uses the PDF.js library.



  151
  
  
 * For all other supported formats: uses the officeParser library.



  152
  
  
 *



  153
  
  
 * @param {File} file



  154
  
  
 * @returns {Promise<string>} Plain text content of the file



  155
  
  
 * @throws {Error} If the file format is unsupported or parsing fails



  156
  
  
 */



  157
  
  
export async function parseFile(file) {



  158
  —
  
  const ext = getExtension(file.name);



  159
  
  



  160
  
  
  // Plain text — no parsing needed



  161
  —
  
  if (ext === ".txt") {



  162
  —
  
    return file.text();



  163
  —
  
  }



  164
  
  



  165
  
  
  // Markdown — read as text and strip syntax for cleaner embeddings



  166
  —
  
  if (ext === ".md") {



  167
  —
  
    const raw = await file.text();



  168
  —
  
    return stripMarkdownSyntax(raw);



  169
  —
  
  }



  170
  
  



  171
  
  
  // PDF — use PDF.js



  172
  —
  
  if (PDF_EXTENSIONS.includes(ext)) {



  173
  —
  
    const buffer = await file.arrayBuffer();



  174
  —
  
    return parsePdf(buffer);



  175
  —
  
  }



  176
  
  



  177
  
  
  // Everything else — use officeParser



  178
  —
  
  if (OFFICE_EXTENSIONS.includes(ext)) {



  179
  —
  
    await loadOfficeParser();



  180
  —
  
    const buffer = await file.arrayBuffer();



  181
  —
  
    // Pass fileType hint so officeParser can parse formats without magic bytes (csv)



  182
  —
  
    return parseWithOfficeParser(buffer, ext.slice(1));



  183
  —
  
  }



  184
  
  



  185
  —
  
  throw new Error(



  186
  —
  
    `Unsupported file format: "${ext}". Supported formats: ${SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS.join(", ")}`,



  187
  —
  
  );



  188
  
  
}



  189
  
  



  190
  
  
/**



  191
  
  
 * Strip Markdown syntax from a text, converting it to plain text.



  192
  
  
 * Handles: headings, bold/italic, links, images, code blocks, lists,



  193
  
  
 * blockquotes, horizontal rules, and HTML tags.



  194
  
  
 *



  195
  
  
 * @param {string} text



  196
  
  
 * @returns {string}



  197
  
  
 */



  198
  
  
function stripMarkdownSyntax(text) {



  199
  —
  
  return (



  200
  —
  
    text



  201
  —
  
      // Remove HTML tags (common in Markdown)



  202
  —
  
      .replace(/<!--[\s\S]*?-->/g, "")



  203
  —
  
      .replace(/<[^>]+>/g, "")



  204
  —
  



  205
  —
  
      // Remove fenced code blocks (``` ... ```)



  206
  —
  
      .replace(/```[\s\S]*?```/g, "")



  207
  —
  



  208
  —
  
      // Remove inline code (`code`)



  209
  —
  
      .replace(/`[^`]+`/g, "")



  210
  —
  



  211
  —
  
      // Remove images (![alt](url))



  212
  —
  
      .replace(/!\[[^\]]*\]\([^)]*\)/g, "")



  213
  —
  



  214
  —
  
      // Remove links — keep only the link text [text](url) → text



  215
  —
  
      .replace(/\[([^\]]*)\]\([^)]*\)/g, "$1")



  216
  —
  



  217
  —
  
      // Remove headings (# Heading)



  218
  —
  
      .replace(/^#{1,6}\s+/gm, "")



  219
  —
  



  220
  —
  
      // Remove horizontal rules (---, ***, ___)



  221
  —
  
      .replace(/^[-*_]{3,}\s*$/gm, "")



  222
  —
  



  223
  —
  
      // Remove blockquote markers (>)



  224
  —
  
      .replace(/^>\s+/gm, "")



  225
  —
  



  226
  —
  
      // Remove list markers (* -, +)



  227
  —
  
      .replace(/^[\s]*[-*+]\s+/gm, "")



  228
  —
  



  229
  —
  
      // Remove ordered list markers (1. 2. etc.)



  230
  —
  
      .replace(/^\d+\.\s+/gm, "")



  231
  —
  



  232
  —
  
      // Remove bold (**text** or __text__)



  233
  —
  
      .replace(/\*\*([^*]+)\*\*/g, "$1")



  234
  —
  
      .replace(/__([^_]+)__/g, "$1")



  235
  —
  



  236
  —
  
      // Remove italic (*text* or _text_)



  237
  —
  
      .replace(/\*([^*]+)\*/g, "$1")



  238
  —
  
      .replace(/_([^_]+)_/g, "$1")



  239
  —
  



  240
  —
  
      // Remove strikethrough (~~text~~)



  241
  —
  
      .replace(/~~([^~]+)~~/g, "$1")



  242
  —
  



  243
  —
  
      // Clean up extra blank lines (collapse 3+ newlines to 2)



  244
  —
  
      .replace(/\n{3,}/g, "\n\n")



  245
  —
  
  );



  246
  
  
}



  247
  
  



  248
  
  
/**



  249
  
  
 * Parse an ArrayBuffer using the globally-loaded officeParser library.



  250
  
  
 *



  251
  
  
 * @param {ArrayBuffer} buffer



  252
  
  
 * @param {string} [fileType] — Explicit file type hint (e.g. "csv") for formats



  253
  
  
 *                              without magic bytes; optional for binary formats.



  254
  
  
 * @returns {Promise<string>}



  255
  
  
 * @throws {Error} If officeParser is not loaded or parsing fails



  256
  
  
 */



  257
  
  
async function parseWithOfficeParser(buffer, fileType) {



  258
  —
  
  if (typeof officeParser === "undefined") {



  259
  —
  
    throw new Error(



  260
  —
  
      "officeParser library failed to load. Check your internet connection.",



  261
  —
  
    );



  262
  —
  
  }



  263
  
  



  264
  —
  
  const config = fileType ? { fileType } : {};



  265
  —
  
  const ast = await officeParser.parseOffice(new Uint8Array(buffer), config);



  266
  —
  
  return ast.toText();



  267
  
  
}



  268
  
  



  269
  
  
// ─── PDF.js Integration ───────────────────────────────────────────────────



  270
  
  



  271
  
  
/**



  272
  
  
 * Load PDF.js from CDN as an ES module. Returns the pdfjsLib namespace.



  273
  
  
 * Lazy-loaded on first demand; subsequent calls reuse the same promise.



  274
  
  
 *



  275
  
  
 * @returns {Promise<object>} The pdfjsLib module namespace



  276
  
  
 */



  277
  
  
async function loadPdfJs() {



  278
  —
  
  if (pdfjsLoadPromise !== null) {



  279
  —
  
    return pdfjsLoadPromise;



  280
  —
  
  }



  281
  
  



  282
  —
  
  pdfjsLoadPromise = (async () => {



  283
  —
  
    try {



  284
  —
  
      const pdfjsLib = await import(



  285
  —
  
        /* webpackIgnore: true */ `${PDFJS_CDN_BASE}/pdf.min.mjs`



  286
  —
  
      );



  287
  —
  
      // Configure the worker from the same CDN version



  288
  —
  
      pdfjsLib.GlobalWorkerOptions.workerSrc = `${PDFJS_CDN_BASE}/pdf.worker.min.mjs`;



  289
  —
  
      return pdfjsLib;



  290
  —
  
    } catch (error) {



  291
  —
  
      pdfjsLoadPromise = null; // Reset so we can retry



  292
  —
  
      throw new Error(



  293
  —
  
        `Failed to load PDF.js from CDN (${PDFJS_CDN_BASE}): ${error.message}`,



  294
  —
  
      );



  295
  —
  
    }



  296
  —
  
  })();



  297
  
  



  298
  —
  
  return pdfjsLoadPromise;



  299
  
  
}



  300
  
  



  301
  
  
/**



  302
  
  
 * Parse a PDF ArrayBuffer and extract its plain text content.



  303
  
  
 * Uses PDF.js to iterate each page and extract text items, joining them



  304
  
  
 * with newlines for readable output.



  305
  
  
 *



  306
  
  
 * @param {ArrayBuffer} buffer - Raw PDF file data



  307
  
  
 * @returns {Promise<string>} Plain text content of the PDF



  308
  
  
 * @throws {Error} If PDF.js fails to load or parsing fails



  309
  
  
 */



  310
  
  
async function parsePdf(buffer) {



  311
  —
  
  const pdfjsLib = await loadPdfJs();



  312
  
  



  313
  —
  
  const loadingTask = pdfjsLib.getDocument({



  314
  —
  
    data: new Uint8Array(buffer),



  315
  —
  
  });



  316
  —
  
  const pdf = await loadingTask.promise;



  317
  
  



  318
  —
  
  const pagesText = [];



  319
  
  



  320
  —
  
  for (let i = 1; i <= pdf.numPages; i++) {



  321
  —
  
    const page = await pdf.getPage(i);



  322
  —
  
    const textContent = await page.getTextContent();



  323
  —
  
    // Extract text strings and join them for this page



  324
  —
  
    const pageText = textContent.items.map((item) => item.str).join("");



  325
  —
  
    if (pageText.trim()) {



  326
  —
  
      pagesText.push(pageText);



  327
  —
  
    }



  328
  —
  
  }



  329
  
  



  330
  
  
  // Join pages with double newline for readable separation



  331
  —
  
  return pagesText.join("\n\n");



  332
  
  
}



  333

// IndexedDB constants

const DB_NAME = "rag-browser-db";

  49

  16
  
  



  17
  
  
/**



  18
  
  
 * Create a new Orama database with the document schema.



  19
  
  
 *



  20
  
  
 * Schema fields:



  21
  
  
 * - content: string (the text chunk)



  22
  
  
 * - embedding: vector[1024] (the embedding vector for similarity search)



  23
  
  
 * - metadata.sourceFile: string (origin filename)



  24
  
  
 * - metadata.chunkIndex: number (sequential index within the file)



  25
  
  
 * - metadata.charOffset: number (character offset in original text)



  26
  
  
 * - metadata.charLength: number (length of this chunk in characters)



  27
  
  
 */



  28
  
  
export function createDB() {



  29
  30
  
  return create({



  30
  30
  
    schema: {



  31
  30
  
      content: "string",



  32
  30
  
      embedding: "vector[1024]",



  33
  30
  
      metadata: {



  34
  30
  
        sourceFile: "string",



  35
  30
  
        chunkIndex: "number",



  36
  30
  
        charOffset: "number",



  37
  30
  
        charLength: "number",



  38
  30
  
      },



  39
  30
  
    },



  40
  30
  
  });



  41
  
  
}



  42
  
  



  43
  
  
/**



  44

  47
  
  
 */

export async function insertChunks(db, chunks) {



  49
  —

  50
  
  
}



  51
  
  



  52
  
  
/**



  53
  
  
 * Perform vector similarity search on the Orama database.



  54
  
  
 *



  55
  
  
 * @param {Object} db - Orama database instance



  56
  
  
 * @param {number[]} queryEmbedding - 1024-dimensional query vector



  57
  
  
 * @param {Object} options - Search options



  58
  
  
 * @param {number} options.similarity - Minimum cosine similarity (default 0.7)



  59
  
  
 * @param {number} options.limit - Max results to return (default 5)



  60
  
  
 * @returns {Object} Search results with hits array



  61
  
  
 */



  62
  
  
export async function searchVector(db, queryEmbedding, options = {}) {



  63
  —
  
  return search(db, {



  64
  —
  
    mode: "vector",



  65
  —
  
    vector: {



  66
  —
  
      value: queryEmbedding,



  67
  —
  
      property: "embedding",



  68
  —
  
    },



  69
  —
  
    similarity: options.similarity ?? 0.7,



  70
  —
  
    limit: options.limit ?? 5,



  71
  —
  
    includeVectors: false,



  72
  —
  
  });



  73
  
  
}



  74
  
  



  75
  
  
/**



  76
  
  
 * Perform hybrid search (BM25 keyword + vector similarity) on the Orama database.



  77
  
  
 *



  78
  
  
 * Hybrid search combines lexical keyword matching with semantic vector similarity,



  79
  
  
 * producing significantly more precise results than pure vector search alone.



  80
  
  
 * This prevents irrelevant chunks from being retrieved when vector similarity



  81
  
  
 * scores are ambiguous (e.g., short documents with few chunks).



  82
  
  
 *



  83
  
  
 * @param {Object} db - Orama database instance



  84
  
  
 * @param {string} queryText - The original user query for keyword matching



  85
  
  
 * @param {number[]} queryEmbedding - 1024-dimensional query vector



  86
  
  
 * @param {Object} options - Search options



  87
  
  
 * @param {number} options.similarity - Minimum combined similarity (default 0.75)



  88
  
  
 * @param {number} options.minVectorSimilarity - Minimum vector similarity gate (default 0.65)



  89
  
  
 * @param {number} options.limit - Max results to return (default 5)



  90
  
  
 * @param {Object} options.hybridWeights - BM25 vs vector weight balance



  91
  
  
 * @returns {Object} Search results with hits array



  92
  
  
 */



  93
  
  
export async function searchHybrid(



  94
  
  
  db,



  95
  
  
  queryText,



  96
  
  
  queryEmbedding,



  97
  
  
  options = {},



  98
  
  
) {



  99
  —
  
  const results = search(db, {



  100
  —
  
    mode: "hybrid",



  101
  —
  
    term: queryText,



  102
  —
  
    vector: {



  103
  —
  
      value: queryEmbedding,



  104
  —
  
      property: "embedding",



  105
  —
  
    },



  106
  —
  
    similarity: options.similarity ?? 0.75,



  107
  —
  
    limit: options.limit ?? 5,



  108
  —
  
    includeVectors: false,



  109
  —
  
    // Weight BM25 keyword matching higher for precision



  110
  —
  
    hybridWeights: options.hybridWeights ?? {



  111
  —
  
      text: 0.7,



  112
  —
  
      vector: 0.3,



  113
  —
  
    },



  114
  —
  
  });



  115
  
  



  116
  
  
  // Quality gate: filter out results with vector similarity below threshold.



  117
  
  
  // Hybrid scoring can promote keyword-only matches with low semantic relevance.



  118
  
  
  // The minVectorSimilarity gate ensures semantic relevance is maintained.



  119
  —
  
  const minVectorSim = options.minVectorSimilarity ?? 0.65;



  120
  —
  
  if (results.hits) {



  121
  —
  
    results.hits = results.hits.filter((hit) => hit.score >= minVectorSim);



  122
  —
  
  }



  123
  
  



  124
  —
  
  return results;



  125
  
  
}

  127
  
  
/**

export function getDocumentCount(db) {

/**



  135
  
  
 * Open an IndexedDB connection with proper upgrade handling.



  136
  
  
 * @returns {Promise<IDBDatabase>} Resolved database connection



  137
  
  
 */



  138
  
  
function openDB() {



  139
  53
  
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {



  140
  53
  
    const req = indexedDB.open(DB_NAME, DB_VERSION);



  141
  53
  
    req.onupgradeneeded = () => {



  142
  4
  
      const db = req.result;



  143
  4
  
      if (!db.objectStoreNames.contains(STORE_NAME)) {



  144
  4
  
        db.createObjectStore(STORE_NAME, { keyPath: "id" });



  145
  4
  
      }



  146
  53
  
    };



  147
  47
  
    req.onsuccess = () => resolve(req.result);



  148
  —
  
    req.onerror = () => reject(req.error);



  149
  53
  
  });



  150
  
  
}



  151
  
  



  152
  
  
/**



  153
  
  
 * Persist the Orama database to IndexedDB.



  154
  
  
 * Uses Orama's save() to produce proper serializable state, then stores it



  155
  
  
 * as a JSON blob alongside metadata.



  156
  
  
 *



  157
  
  
 * @param {Object} db - Orama database instance



  158
  
  
 */



  159
  
  
export async function persistIndex(db) {



  160
  4
  
  try {



  161
  4
  
    const rawData = save(db);



  162
  4
  
    const data = JSON.stringify(rawData);



  163
  4
  
    const blob = new Blob([data], { type: "application/json" });



  164
  4
  



  165
  4
  
    const idb = await openDB();



  166
  4
  
    const tx = idb.transaction([STORE_NAME], "readwrite");



  167
  4
  
    const store = tx.objectStore(STORE_NAME);



  168
  4
  
    store.clear();



  169
  4
  
    store.put({ id: "orama-db", data: blob });



  170
  4
  
    store.put({



  171
  4
  
      id: "metadata",



  172
  4
  
      timestamp: Date.now(),



  173
  4
  
      version: DB_VERSION,



  174
  4
  
    });



  175
  4
  



  176
  4
  
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {



  177
  4
  
      tx.oncomplete = () => resolve();



  178
  —
  
      tx.onerror = () => reject(tx.error);



  179
  4
  
    });



  180
  4
  
  } catch (error) {



  181
  —
  
    if (error.name === "QuotaExceededError") {



  182
  —
  
      console.warn(



  183
  —
  
        "IndexedDB quota exceeded. Consider reducing document count.",



  184
  —
  
      );



  185
  —
  
    }



  186
  —
  
    throw error;



  187
  4
  
  }



  188
  
  
}

  190
  
  
/**



  191
  
  
 * Restore the Orama database from IndexedDB.

  193

  194

  195
  
  
 * method. This preserves backward compatibility with previously persisted



  196
  
  
 * data after the version bump.

 */

export async function restoreIndex() {



  201
  49
  
  try {



  202
  49
  
    const idb = await openDB();



  203
  43
  
    const tx = idb.transaction([STORE_NAME], "readonly");



  204
  43
  
    const store = tx.objectStore(STORE_NAME);



  205
  49
  



  206
  43
  
    const req = store.get("orama-db");



  207
  43
  
    return new Promise((resolve) => {



  208
  43
  
      req.onsuccess = async () => {



  209
  30
  
        if (req.result && req.result.data) {



  210
  25
  
          const text = await req.result.data.text();



  211
  21
  
          const storedData = JSON.parse(text);



  212
  30
  



  213
  30
  
          // Detect format and restore appropriately



  214
  21
  
          if (storedData.schema) {



  215
  21
  
            // Old format: raw DB object — extract docs and re-insert



  216
  —

  217
  —
  
            let docs = storedData.docs;



  218
  —
  
            if (docs && typeof docs === "object" && !Array.isArray(docs)) {

  —

  221
  —
  
            if (Array.isArray(docs) && docs.length > 0) {

  —
  
              await insertMultiple(db, docs);



  223
  —
  
            }



  224
  —
  
            resolve(db);



  225
  21
  
          } else {



  226
  21
  
            // New format from save(): use load() for instant restoration



  227
  21
  
            const db = createDB();



  228
  21
  
            await load(db, storedData);



  229
  21
  
            resolve(db);



  230
  21
  
          }



  231
  30
  
        } else {

  5
  
          resolve(createDB());



  233
  30
  
        }



  234
  43
  
      };



  235
  —
  
      req.onerror = () => resolve(createDB());



  236
  43
  
    });



  237
  49
  
  } catch {



  238
  —
  
    return createDB();



  239
  49
  
  }



  240
  
  
}

// ─── Default database ──────────────────────────────────────────────

  279
  
  
// ─── Export / Import helpers ────────────────────────────────────────



  280
  
  



  281
  49
  
const EXPORT_FORMAT = "rag-browser-orama";



  282
  49
  
const EXPORT_VERSION = 2;



  283
  49
  
const EXPECTED_DIMENSION = 1024;



  284
  
  



  285
  
  
/**



  286
  
  
 * Serialize the Orama database into a versioned JSON envelope suitable for



  287
  
  
 * file export. Uses Orama's save() to produce proper serializable state,



  288
  
  
 * ensuring all internal structures (indices, vectors) are captured faithfully.



  289
  
  
 *



  290
  
  
 * @param {Object} db - Orama database instance



  291
  
  
 * @returns {Blob} JSON blob ready for download



  292
  
  
 */



  293
  
  
export function serializeDB(db) {



  294
  —
  
  const rawData = save(db);



  295
  
  



  296
  —
  
  const envelope = {



  297
  —
  
    format: EXPORT_FORMAT,



  298
  —
  
    version: EXPORT_VERSION,



  299
  —
  
    exportedAt: new Date().toISOString(),



  300
  —
  
    embeddingDimension: EXPECTED_DIMENSION,



  301
  —
  
    documentCount: count(db),



  302
  —
  
    rawData: rawData,



  303
  —
  
  };



  304
  
  



  305
  —
  
  return new Blob([JSON.stringify(envelope)], {



  306
  —
  
    type: "application/json",



  307
  —
  
  });



  308
  
  
}



  309
  
  



  310
  
  
/**



  311
  
  
 * Generate a human-friendly filename for an exported database.



  312
  
  
 * Pattern: rag-browser-db-YYYYMMDD-HHmmss-Nchunks.json



  313
  
  
 *



  314
  
  
 * @param {number} documentCount - Number of chunks in the database



  315
  
  
 * @returns {string}



  316
  
  
 */



  317
  
  
export function generateExportFilename(documentCount) {



  318
  —
  
  const now = new Date();



  319
  —
  
  const pad = (n, w = 2) => String(n).padStart(w, "0");



  320
  —
  
  const ts = `${now.getFullYear()}${pad(now.getMonth() + 1)}${pad(now.getDate())}-${pad(now.getHours())}${pad(now.getMinutes())}${pad(now.getSeconds())}`;



  321
  —
  
  return `rag-browser-db-${ts}-${documentCount}chunks.json`;



  322
  
  
}



  323
  
  



  324
  
  
/**



  325
  
  
 * Restore an Orama database from serialized raw data.



  326
  
  
 *



  327
  
  
 * Detects the data format and uses the appropriate restoration method:



  328
  
  
 * - New format (from save()): has searchablePropertiesWithTypes → use load()



  329
  
  
 * - Old format (raw DB object): has schema + docs → extract and re-insert



  330
  
  
 *



  331
  
  
 * @param {Object} rawData - The raw serialized state from save() or an old-style database object



  332
  
  
 * @returns {Promise<Object>} A fully functional Orama database instance



  333
  
  
 */



  334
  
  
export async function restoreFromData(rawData) {



  335
  4
  
  const db = createDB();



  336
  
  



  337
  4
  
  if (rawData.index?.searchablePropertiesWithTypes) {



  338
  4
  
    // New format: produced by save() — load restores the full internal state



  339
  4
  
    await load(db, rawData);



  340
  —
  
  } else if (rawData.docs && typeof rawData.docs === "object") {



  341
  —
  
    // Old format (raw DB object or version 1 export): extract documents and re-insert.



  342
  —
  
    // Docs may be an array or an object keyed by internal ID (e.g. {"1": {...}, "2": {...}}).



  343
  —
  
    let docs = rawData.docs;



  344
  —
  
    if (!Array.isArray(docs) && docs.docs) {



  345
  —
  
      // Nested docs structure: { docs: { ... }, count: N }



  346
  —
  
      docs = docs.docs;



  347
  —
  
    }



  348
  —
  
    const docArray = Array.isArray(docs) ? docs : Object.values(docs);



  349
  —
  
    if (docArray.length > 0) {



  350
  —
  
      await insertMultiple(db, docArray);



  351
  —
  
    }



  352
  —
  
  }



  353
  
  



  354
  4
  
  return db;



  355
  
  
}



  356
  
  



  357
  
  
/**



  358
  
  
 * Validate an imported database file. Accepts both the new versioned envelope



  359
  
  
 * format (with rawData) and old formats for backward compatibility:



  360
  
  
 * - Version 2: envelope with 'rawData' key (save() output, no top-level schema)



  361
  
  
 * - Version 1: envelope with 'database' key (raw Orama object with schema)



  362
  
  
 * - Raw Orama object: direct schema/docs structure



  363
  
  
 *



  364
  
  
 * @param {Object} data - Parsed JSON from the uploaded file



  365
  
  
 * @returns {{ valid: boolean, rawData?: Object, metadata?: Object, error?: string }}



  366
  
  
 */



  367
  
  
export function validateImport(data) {



  368
  4
  
  if (!data || typeof data !== "object") {



  369
  —
  
    return { valid: false, error: "File does not contain valid JSON." };



  370
  4
  
  }



  371
  
  



  372
  4
  
  let rawData = null;



  373
  4
  
  let metadata = {};



  374
  
  



  375
  
  
  // Detect format and extract raw database data



  376
  4
  
  if (data.format === EXPORT_FORMAT && data.rawData) {



  377
  4
  
    // Version 2: proper serialized state from save()



  378
  4
  
    rawData = data.rawData;



  379
  4
  
    metadata = {



  380
  4
  
      format: data.format,



  381
  4
  
      version: data.version,



  382
  4
  
      exportedAt: data.exportedAt,



  383
  4
  
      embeddingDimension: data.embeddingDimension,



  384
  4
  
      documentCount: data.documentCount,



  385
  4
  
    };



  386
  —
  
  } else if (data.format === EXPORT_FORMAT && data.database) {



  387
  —
  
    // Version 1: raw Orama object (backward compatibility)



  388
  —
  
    rawData = data.database;



  389
  —
  
    metadata = {



  390
  —
  
      format: data.format,



  391
  —
  
      version: data.version,



  392
  —
  
      exportedAt: data.exportedAt,



  393
  —
  
      embeddingDimension: data.embeddingDimension,



  394
  —
  
      documentCount: data.documentCount,



  395
  —
  
    };



  396
  —
  
  } else if (data.schema && data.docs) {



  397
  —
  
    // Raw Orama object (backward compatibility)



  398
  —
  
    rawData = data;



  399
  —
  
    metadata = { format: "raw-orama" };



  400
  —
  
  } else {



  401
  —
  
    return {



  402
  —
  
      valid: false,



  403
  —
  
      error:



  404
  —
  
        "File is not a valid RAG-Browser database export. Expected a JSON file with 'format' and 'rawData' keys, or a raw Orama database.",



  405
  —
  
    };



  406
  —
  
  }



  407
  
  



  408
  
  
  // Validate structure based on format type.



  409
  
  
  // save() output nests searchablePropertiesWithTypes under index.



  410
  
  
  // Raw DB objects use a nested schema object.



  411
  4
  
  if (rawData.index?.searchablePropertiesWithTypes) {



  412
  4
  
    const types = rawData.index.searchablePropertiesWithTypes;



  413
  4
  



  414
  4
  
    if (types.content !== "string") {



  415
  —
  
      return {



  416
  —
  
        valid: false,



  417
  —
  
        error: 'Schema mismatch: expected content field of type "string".',



  418
  —
  
      };



  419
  4
  
    }



  420
  4
  



  421
  4
  
    const embType = types.embedding;



  422
  4
  
    const dimMatch = String(embType).match(/^vector\[(\d+)\]$/);



  423
  4
  
    if (!dimMatch) {



  424
  —
  
      return {



  425
  —
  
        valid: false,



  426
  —
  
        error:



  427
  —
  
          'Schema mismatch: expected an embedding field like "vector[1024]".',



  428
  —
  
      };



  429
  4
  
    }



  430
  4
  



  431
  4
  
    const actualDim = parseInt(dimMatch[1], 10);



  432
  4
  
    if (actualDim !== EXPECTED_DIMENSION) {



  433
  —
  
      return {



  434
  —
  
        valid: false,



  435
  —
  
        error: `Embedding dimension mismatch. Expected ${EXPECTED_DIMENSION}, got ${actualDim}. This database was likely created with a different embedding model.`,



  436
  —
  
      };



  437
  4
  
    }



  438
  4
  



  439
  4
  
    if (!types["metadata.sourceFile"] || !types["metadata.chunkIndex"]) {



  440
  —
  
      return {



  441
  —
  
        valid: false,



  442
  —
  
        error: 'Schema mismatch: expected "metadata" nested fields.',



  443
  —
  
      };



  444
  4
  
    }



  445
  —
  
  } else if (rawData.schema && typeof rawData.schema === "object") {



  446
  —
  
    // Old-format or raw Orama object validation



  447
  —
  
    const schema = rawData.schema;



  448
  —
  



  449
  —
  
    if (schema.content !== "string") {



  450
  —
  
      return {



  451
  —
  
        valid: false,



  452
  —
  
        error: 'Schema mismatch: expected content field of type "string".',



  453
  —
  
      };



  454
  —
  
    }



  455
  —
  



  456
  —
  
    const embType = schema.embedding;



  457
  —
  
    const dimMatch = String(embType).match(/^vector\[(\d+)\]$/);



  458
  —
  
    if (!dimMatch) {



  459
  —
  
      return {



  460
  —
  
        valid: false,



  461
  —
  
        error:



  462
  —
  
          'Schema mismatch: expected an embedding field like "vector[1024]".',



  463
  —
  
      };



  464
  —
  
    }



  465
  —
  



  466
  —
  
    const actualDim = parseInt(dimMatch[1], 10);



  467
  —
  
    if (actualDim !== EXPECTED_DIMENSION) {



  468
  —
  
      return {



  469
  —
  
        valid: false,



  470
  —
  
        error: `Embedding dimension mismatch. Expected ${EXPECTED_DIMENSION}, got ${actualDim}. This database was likely created with a different embedding model.`,



  471
  —
  
      };



  472
  —
  
    }



  473
  —
  



  474
  —
  
    const metaSchema = schema.metadata;



  475
  —
  
    if (!metaSchema || typeof metaSchema !== "object") {



  476
  —
  
      return {



  477
  —
  
        valid: false,



  478
  —
  
        error: 'Schema mismatch: expected a "metadata" nested field.',



  479
  —
  
      };



  480
  —
  
    }



  481
  —
  
  } else {



  482
  —
  
    return {



  483
  —
  
      valid: false,



  484
  —
  
      error:



  485
  —
  
        "Database is missing a recognizable structure. Expected either 'searchablePropertiesWithTypes' (new format) or 'schema' (old format).",



  486
  —
  
    };



  487
  —
  
  }



  488
  
  



  489
  4
  
  return { valid: true, rawData, metadata };



  490
  
  
}



  491

// state.js — Central application state with observable pattern

  10
  
  
/**



  11

  12

  13
  
  
 * - models: loading status of embedding and LLM models



  14

  15

  16
  
  
 */



  17
  
  
/**



  18
  
  
 * Default search configuration.



  19
  
  
 */



  20
  
  
/**



  21
  
  
 * Default LLM configuration.



  22
  
  
 *



  23
  
  
 * Generation parameters follow official Qwen3.5 recommendations from



  24
  
  
 * unsloth.ai/docs/models/qwen3.5.



  25
  
  
 *



  26
  
  
 * Non-thinking (general tasks): temp=0.7, top_p=0.8, top_k=20,



  27
  
  
 *   min_p=0.0, presence=1.5, repeat=1.0



  28
  
  
 * Thinking (general tasks):    temp=1.0, top_p=0.95, top_k=20,



  29
  
  
 *   min_p=0.0, presence=1.5, repeat=1.0



  30
  
  
 */



  31
  49
  
const NON_THINKING_PRESET = {



  32
  49
  
  temperature: 0.7,



  33
  49
  
  top_p: 0.8,



  34
  49
  
  top_k: 20,



  35
  49
  
  min_p: 0.0,



  36
  49
  
  presence_penalty: 1.5,



  37
  49
  
  repetition_penalty: 1.0,



  38
  49
  
  max_new_tokens: 8192,



  39
  49
  
};



  40
  
  



  41
  49
  
const THINKING_PRESET = {



  42
  49
  
  temperature: 1.0,



  43
  49
  
  top_p: 0.95,



  44
  49
  
  top_k: 20,



  45
  49
  
  min_p: 0.0,



  46
  49
  
  presence_penalty: 1.5,



  47
  49
  
  repetition_penalty: 1.0,



  48
  49
  
  max_new_tokens: 8192,



  49
  49
  
};



  50
  
  



  51
  
  
/**



  52
  
  
 * Default maximum thinking tokens for Qwen3.5's reasoning mode.



  53
  
  
 * Controls the token budget allocated to the model's internal reasoning.



  54
  
  
 * Defaults to half of the thinking preset's max_new_tokens.



  55
  
  
 * Valid range: 1024–8192.



  56
  
  
 */



  57
  49
  
const DEFAULT_MAX_THINKING_TOKENS = Math.floor(



  58
  49
  
  THINKING_PRESET.max_new_tokens / 2,



  59
  49
  
);



  60
  
  



  61
  
  
/**



  62

  63
  
  
 * While Qwen3.5-2B supports 262K tokens natively, WebGPU memory constraints



  64
  
  
 * in the browser limit the practical context window to ~32K tokens.



  65
  
  
 */



  66
  49
  
const DEFAULT_CONTEXT_WINDOW = 32768;



  67
  
  



  68
  49
  
export const DEFAULT_LLM_CONFIG = {



  69
  49
  
  enableThinking: false, // Qwen3.5-2B defaults to non-thinking mode



  70
  49
  
  maxThinkingTokens: DEFAULT_MAX_THINKING_TOKENS,



  71
  49
  
  generation: { ...NON_THINKING_PRESET },



  72
  49

  73
  
  



  74
  
  
/**

  78

export function getGenerationPreset(enableThinking) {



  80

  81
  
  
}



  82
  
  



  83
  
  
/**



  84
  
  
 * Default search configuration.



  85
  
  
 */



  86
  49
  
export const DEFAULT_SEARCH_CONFIG = {



  87
  49
  
  mode: "hybrid", // 'hybrid' | 'vector'



  88
  49
  
  hybridWeights: {

  49
  
    // BM25 vs vector balance (only in hybrid mode)

  49
  
  },

  100
  
  



  101
  49
  
let state = {



  102
  49
  
  hardware: {



  103
  49
  
    webgpuAvailable: false,

  49
  
    device: "wasm",

  49

  49

  124
  49

  126

  127

  130

  131

  132
  
  



  133
  
  
/**

  141

  142
  
  



  143
  
  
/**



  144
  
  
 * Subscribe to state changes.



  145

  146

  147

  148
  
  
export function subscribe(fn) {



  149
  52
  
  subscribers.push(fn);



  150
  52
  
  return () => {



  151

    if (idx !== -1) subscribers.splice(idx, 1);



  153
  52
  
  };



  154
  
  
}

  156
  
  
/**



  157

export function addMessage(role, content, contextChunks = null) {

  —
  
  const message = {



  164
  —
  
    id: generateUUID(),



  165
  —
  
    role,



  166
  —

  167
  —

  168
  —

  169
  —
  
  };



  170
  —

  171
  —

  172
  
  
}



  173
  
  



  174
  
  
/**



  175
  
  
 * Clear the entire conversation history.

export function clearConversation() {

/**

  188

  —

  —

}

export function setSearchConfig(updates) {

  —

  —

  2
  
  }

  2
  
  for (const key of ["mode", "topN"]) {

    if (updates[key] !== undefined) {

      state.searchConfig[key] = updates[key];



  214
  2

  215
  2
  
  }

  2
  
  subscribers.forEach((fn) => fn(state));

}

  219
  
  
/**



  220
  
  
 * Reset search configuration to defaults.

export function resetSearchConfig() {

  227
  
  
/**



  228
  
  
 * Update LLM configuration. Merges partial config into existing state.



  229
  
  
 * Supports updating individual generation parameters via `generation` key.



  230
  
  
 * @param {Object} updates - Partial LLM config to merge



  231
  
  
 */



  232
  
  
export function setLlmConfig(updates) {



  233
  1
  
  for (const key of ["enableThinking"]) {



  234
  1
  
    if (updates[key] !== undefined) {



  235
  —
  
      state.llmConfig[key] = updates[key];



  236
  1
  
      // Auto-apply generation preset when thinking mode changes



  237
  —
  
      state.llmConfig.generation = {



  238
  —
  
        ...getGenerationPreset(state.llmConfig.enableThinking),



  239
  —
  
      };



  240
  1
  
      // Reset maxThinkingTokens to default when enabling thinking mode



  241
  —
  
      if (state.llmConfig.enableThinking) {



  242
  —
  
        state.llmConfig.maxThinkingTokens = DEFAULT_MAX_THINKING_TOKENS;



  243
  —
  
      }



  244
  1
  
    }



  245
  1
  
  }



  246
  
  
  // Handle direct maxThinkingTokens updates (e.g., from slider)



  247
  1
  
  if (updates.maxThinkingTokens !== undefined) {



  248
  —
  
    state.llmConfig.maxThinkingTokens = updates.maxThinkingTokens;



  249
  1
  
  }



  250
  1
  
  if (updates.generation !== undefined) {



  251
  1
  
    state.llmConfig.generation = {



  252
  1
  
      ...state.llmConfig.generation,



  253
  1
  
      ...updates.generation,



  254
  1
  
    };



  255
  1
  
  }



  256
  1
  
  subscribers.forEach((fn) => fn(state));



  257
  
  
}



  258
  
  



  259
  
  
/**



  260
  
  
 * Reset LLM configuration to defaults.



  261
  
  
 */



  262
  
  
export function resetLlmConfig() {



  263
  —
  
  state.llmConfig = { ...DEFAULT_LLM_CONFIG };



  264
  —
  
  subscribers.forEach((fn) => fn(state));



  265
  
  
}



  266
  
  



  267
  
  
/**



  268
  
  
 * Reset only the generation parameters to the preset for the current thinking mode.



  269
  
  
 * Preserves the current enableThinking setting.



  270
  
  
 */



  271
  
  
export function resetGenerationToPreset() {



  272
  —
  
  state.llmConfig.generation = {



  273
  —
  
    ...getGenerationPreset(state.llmConfig.enableThinking),



  274
  —
  
  };



  275
  —
  
  subscribers.forEach((fn) => fn(state));



  276
  
  
}



  277
  
  



  278
  
  
// ─── Token Tracking ─────────────────────────────────────────────────



  279
  
  



  280
  
  
export function updateTokenTracking(inputTokens, outputTokens) {



  281
  —
  
  const total = inputTokens + outputTokens;



  282
  —
  
  const remaining = state.tokenTracking.contextWindow - total;



  283
  
  



  284
  —
  
  state.tokenTracking = {



  285
  —
  
    ...state.tokenTracking,



  286
  —
  
    inputTokens,



  287
  —
  
    outputTokens,



  288
  —
  
    totalTokens: total,



  289
  —
  
    remainingTokens: remaining,



  290
  —
  
    warningLevel: getWarningLevel(remaining, state.tokenTracking.contextWindow),



  291
  —
  
  };



  292
  —
  
  subscribers.forEach((fn) => fn(state));



  293
  
  
}



  294
  
  



  295
  
  
export function getTokenTracking() {



  296
  —
  
  return { ...state.tokenTracking };



  297
  
  
}



  298
  
  



  299
  
  
export function resetTokenTracking() {



  300
  —
  
  state.tokenTracking = {



  301
  —
  
    contextWindow: state.tokenTracking.contextWindow,



  302
  —
  
    inputTokens: 0,



  303
  —
  
    outputTokens: 0,



  304
  —
  
    totalTokens: 0,



  305
  —
  
    remainingTokens: state.tokenTracking.contextWindow,



  306
  —
  
    warningLevel: "ok",



  307
  —
  
  };



  308
  —
  
  subscribers.forEach((fn) => fn(state));



  309
  
  
}



  310
  
  



  311
  
  
export function setContextWindow(windowSize) {



  312
  —
  
  state.tokenTracking.contextWindow = windowSize;



  313
  —
  
  state.tokenTracking.remainingTokens =



  314
  —
  
    windowSize - state.tokenTracking.totalTokens;



  315
  —
  
  state.tokenTracking.warningLevel = getWarningLevel(



  316
  —
  
    state.tokenTracking.remainingTokens,



  317
  —
  
    windowSize,



  318
  —
  
  );



  319
  —
  
  subscribers.forEach((fn) => fn(state));



  320
  
  
}
